programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: All you need in the kitchen for your precision slicing and dicing of fruits and vegetables. Including three cutting inserts, a finger protector, blade holder and a free docking station.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan's insufferable sister discovers a dead estate estate agent in her new house - and the medical sleuth's investigation throws up no shortage of suspects. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A successful boxer is found murdered shortly after a bout, so Jack steps into the ring to catch the culprit. Detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble finds himself drawn into the struggle for domination of a household between a daughter and her step-mother

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A woman is hired by a shady private detective to impersonate a client, but when he's found dead, she and her aunt find themselves involved in a murder.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Assassins try to kill Walker and the key witness he is escorting by blowing up their plane

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Danny investigate the case of a building worker who died in an underground explosion. An X-ray reveals about 200 of the victim's bones are broken and the autopsy shows he succumbed to asthmatic asphyxiation before the blast. Meanwhile, Stella and Aiden examine the death of a teenager found strangled and floating in a creek

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam and the team spring into action when fellow agent Dom activates a distress signal. They track down his car, which is riddled with bullet holes, and Eric intercepts a signal from his phone, tracing it to a house that contains nothing but some used medical supplies and a dead body. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble is forced into transporting a couple from the mainland to a Florida Key, despite hurricane warnings

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Stone and Robinette investigate when a news reporter is gang-raped by three boys, but have trouble finding enough evidence to prosecute the assailants, so ask Logan and Greevey to reopen the case

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Vance charges DiNozzo with a special assignment to go to France, locate Admiral Kendall's wayward daughter Amanda and bring her home. Arriving in Marseille, Tony immediately heads to the local NCIS office, only to find five dead agents. After eventually tracking down Amanda, the pair embark on a rollercoaster ride to the relative safety of the nearest NCIS office in Spain. Michael Weatherly stars

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A gang of violent drug dealers come looking for a stolen bag of money at the weekend retreat where Walker is staying with a group of underprivileged teenagers

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs travels to his home town after hearing news of his father's death, while the team investigates whether a fire on a US Navy ship was accidental or planned. The vessel served as prison to several members of the Brotherhood of Doubt terrorist group, whom Gibbs was responsible for catching. After the blaze, two of those members are missing. Tony argues that Gibbs would want to know, but Vance does not want him disturbed at this difficult time. Mark Harmon stars

programme.desc: A US marine visits the home of his former captain, only to find the man has been killed, and the killer is waiting inside the property. The subsequent chain of evidence leads Gibbs and Bishop to Afghanistan

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Confidential files are leaked by an anonymous hacker, who threatens to release more unless the Navy agrees to renew a contract with a struggling shipbuilding firm. The Navy Secretary suspects the company's owner Philip Wickes is to blame, and asks NCIS to join forces with the PsyOps unit in an elaborate ruse to bring the culprit out of hiding. Meanwhile, Gibbs' colleagues begin to notice his attraction to his opposite number Samantha Ryan. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Vance's brother-in-law Michael Thomas is linked to the murder of a petty officer, and Gibbs is placed under pressure to clear the man's name. Meanwhile, a negative performance review leaves Tony feeling downhearted and he resolves to prove the assessment wrong by being less chatty while on duty. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine lieutenant's body falls from the sky and the team links the man's death to the disappearance of a Navy chaplain thought to have been kidnapped by rebels working with Colombian crime cartels. Ziva and Tony are dispatched to conduct a rescue operation and enlist the help of former Interpol agent Monique Lisson, while Gibbs discovers there was more to the chaplain's outreach mission than meets the eye. Crime drama, guest starring Karina Lombard (The L Word) and Jamie Lee Curtis (A Fish Called Wanda)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A fire crew discovers a man's body after putting out a raging blaze at a warehouse belonging to a Navy-contracted electronics company, and Gibbs recognises the victim as Carter Plimpton - a former member of an NCIS black-ops unit. Meanwhile, a member of the Baltimore police department helping with the case appears to have a grudge against Tony

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's mission to track the arsonist targeting US Navy facilities appears to end in success when the man in question is killed by a thermite explosion on board the USS Brewer. Meanwhile, Abby studies evidence relating to fire-starter Bruce Johnson, who died when his car exploded, and discovers coded messages in his quarters that indicate he may have been working as part of a larger gang. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony tries to piece together the events that led to him lying wounded in a hospital bed, having been shot in the chest through his bulletproof vest. programme.desc: Christmas special from 1995. The Meldrews' wedding anniversary celebrations turn sour as they learn cousin Ursula has died while watching Noel Edmonds - forcing Victor to defy a witch's grim warning and set out to put her affairs in order

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: The shopkeeping comedy returns with a festive instalment. Granville and Gastric find themselves playing marriage counsellors, while Leroy adopts a festive disguise to avoid relationship trouble of a different kind. Meanwhile, Mrs Featherstone struggles to get into the Christmas spirit and Kath attempts to bring the street together as one harmonious choir of carollers to beat Finkle Street once and for all - but does Granville have a trick up his sleeve to help them out? Starring David Jason, Tim Healy, James Baxter and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Jared discovers a severed human hand in a crayfish pot, begging the questions who does it belong to and are they still alive? Detective drama, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty investigates the apparent suicide of Robert's uncle, found hanged at his home, and discovers the whole village where he lived is gripped by superstition. Hetty begins to suspect that the villagers of Readsby - once a notorious haven for witches - are still engaged in satanic practices, and is determined to prove that they were somehow involved

programme.title: Rillington Place
programme.desc: John Reginald Christie and his estranged wife Ethel are reunited after a nine year separation and move into 10 Rillington Place. As war breaks out, the fractures in their relationship begin to reappear, with deceit, suspicion and violence never far below the veneer of married respectability. Fact-based drama, starring Tim Roth and Samantha Morton

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary invites the others to join him at a hotel in Worthing where he is attending a security conference, but sharing a cramped room between the four of them is not the best way to keep relations on an even keel. If that were not bad enough, Gary falls for one of the delegates - just when Dorothy thinks she might be pregnant. Comedy, with Martin Clunes and Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor returns to New Earth, only for Martha to be kidnapped as soon as they arrive. His search for her leads to a vast underground motorway, where the population of a city is trapped in an endless journey and monsters lurk in the smog. Guest starring Ardal O'Hanlon (Father Ted) and Lenora Crichlow (Being Human)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of two. The Doctor investigates disappearances in New York at the height of the Depression in the 1930s. Monstrous Pig Men linger in the sewers and the Daleks prepare an audacious plan at the top of the Empire State Building. Sci-fi drama, guest starring Hugh Quarshie and Andrew Garfield

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. Dalek Sec is reborn in human form and plans to create a new race of hybrids in 1930s New York. As Sec's human side takes hold, the Doctor sees a chance to permanently alter the evil nature of his old enemies - but the other Daleks have plans of their own. Andrew Garfield and Hugh Quarshie guest star

programme.title: The White Princess
programme.desc: A pretender to the throne is held captive at court, confounding the Tudors, and creating a new rift in Lizzie and King Henry's marriage. Lizzie faces an impossible choice, and makes a risky move to try to eliminate the threat to her family. Jodie Comer, Jacob Collins-Levy, Suki Waterhouse and Michelle Fairley star

programme.desc: Pulp frontman Jarvis Cocker and the Happy Mondays' Bez take part in this special edition for BBC Music Day. Along with their bandmates Candida Doyle and Rowetta Satchell, they join experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey scouring Kent Showground for items that will make them the most profit for charity. While they're busy, presenter Charlie Ross pays a visit to a recording studio in Wales, Rockfields

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine's self-doubt is addressed by an actor, and a missed deadline puts Kam under pressure

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Katharine Merry, Derek Redmond, Ryan Sidebottom, Paul Robinson and Peter Shilton form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes property shopping in Exmoor with a newlywed couple who have a £625k budget for a home that will house his parents and provide her with land for horses

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Head housekeeper Grace, telemarketing managing director Pierre and vineyard manager Adderley spend a day doing each other's jobs before judging whose is the hardest

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth tour the West Country, where Roo meets Exmoor's equine inhabitants and Angus bares all at an exclusive beach that can only be accessed by tunnel

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley with a report on the counterfeit contact lenses that can make the wearer's eyesight worse, and the fake waste disposal companies that could cost consumers a fortune. Michelle also shows how to identify fake emails and dangerous links online, and reveals how criminals can even embed fake messages into a legitimate text chain on a phone

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering some of the most entertaining team challenges - and how different dynamics can lead to either success or chaos in the kitchen. They also revisit a 2015 semi-final task which saw Sheree Murphy, Scott Maslen, Rylan Clark-Neal, Sam Nixon, Chesney Hawkes, Mica Paris, Kimberly Wyatt and Tom Parker thrown together in a Victorian-style village, using only ingredients widely available during that era

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and Martha's romantic weekend on a remote island is cut short when the hotel owner is stabbed to death in his room in what appears to be an attempted robbery. But since the flower beds outside the window are undisturbed, Humphrey is certain that one of the victim's associates played a part in his death. There's just one problem - since the DI had a direct view of the only staircase leading up to the room, he is the alibi for all the suspects. Meanwhile, JP worries about his marriage and seeks relationship advice from Dwayne

programme.title: Can Sex Offenders Change?
programme.desc: Becky Southworth, the daughter of a convicted sex offender, investigates the world of sex offender rehabilitation. Filming across the UK in anonymous locations, she talks to a number of men living in the community, asking what drove them to their horrific crimes, and finding out what treatment is available to stop them reoffending

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police mount urgent searches for two people with dementia. Whilst out shopping with his wife, 83-year-old John turned his back for a moment, before realising that his 82-year-old wife Sheila has vanished on the busy streets of Gloucester town centre. After a frantic search around town, John calls Gloucestershire Police and a major search operation swings into action. Elsewhere in rural Gloucestershire, police scour lanes and wooded areas as they search for 79-year-old John, who disappeared from the home he shares with his wife, Marian when she was out visiting a family member for lunch

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle find out what happens when a silverback gorilla has his authority threatened and meet a new pancake tortoise. Jean Johansson falls for a fennec fox who's looking for a partner

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The team explores a beautiful period cottage in Seend in Wiltshire, a semi in Neath in south Wales and a fire-damaged property in Wolverhampton, which have been sold at auction

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dom helps track down fugitives with a bounty on their heads as he works as a private investigator in Waco, Texas. His mentor is James Fullner, a private investigator who's spent 15 years in the business, exposing cheating partners and business fraud, and apprehending fugitives on the run. Working alongside an armed team of investigators, on behalf of a local bail bondsman, they hit the streets in pursuit of a violent fugitive, a runaway drug addict and a wanted sex offender

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items at Liverpool's St George's Hall, while presenter Eric Knowles finds out why the building came to be built in the city

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Rose shows Kam there is a world beyond school. Meanwhile, it seems Ash may have reached the end of the road with Helena

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team from the world of arts and entertainment comprising actor Simon Greenall, singer Toyah Willcox, arts correspondent Nick Higham, award-winning saxophonist YolanDa Brown and Proms presenter Katie Derham set out to triumph over the general knowledge Goliaths and win the cash prize for charity. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the East Midlands to help a couple find a comfortable country home that brings them closer to family for £450,000. While in the region, Jonnie visits the Leicester Line of the Grand Union canal, a stretch where narrow boats have to navigate a total of 59 locks. He tries his hand at lock keeper's duties, including operating the floodgates

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Climbing guide Jack, master tea-taster Sebastian and luxury couture retailer Onita go head to head in a bid to prove their job is the hardest and claim a £1,000 prize. Day one is steep challenge for Onita as she finds out how many times she's expected to climb one of London's most iconic tourist attractions. The next day sees Jack pushed for time as he grapples with the complexity of grading and blending tea leaves. On the final day, Jack and Sebastian try to master the science of window dressing and selling high-end women's clothing in a luxury boutique

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine enjoys the fun of the fair, while Angus Ashworth heads to Plymouth to discover the turbulent history of gin, before having a go as a Master Distiller. They then join up at their final auction in Clevedon, north Somerset

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones is joined by co-host Iain Stirling to round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: A fed up Miranda makes a huge decision - she packs her bags to go travelling and this time it's for good. Stevie is devastated, while Penny is too wrapped up in renewing her wedding vows to notice. It seems only Gary can stop her going - but how does he really feel? Miranda Hart, Patricia Hodge, Tom Ellis and Sarah Hadland star in the hit comedy

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Documentary looking at how the quiz show has evolved since it was first shown in January 1970 to remain successful in the present day. Archive footage includes when 19 million people tuned in to see the Princess Royal line-up alongside captain Emlyn Hughes, and guests relive their memories of appearing alongside past hosts David Vine and David Coleman, as well as current incumbent Sue Barker. Among the many contributors are Sebastian Coe, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Alan Shearer, Denise Lewis, Gary Lineker, Gabby Logan and current captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Bob Mortimer, Gabby Logan, Katherine Parkinson and Steve Backshall to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: There's a commotion in the housing estate, and Conor and Jock discover can-trailers - tearaways from other estates with open paint cans tied around bikes intent on destroying the rival estate. The lads stop their rivals, but the encounter leads to an argument and Jock is challenged to a boxing match - which he reluctantly agrees to. The duo soon discover his prospective opponent is a champion fighter, meaning Jock has to embark on an extensive training programme in order to sharpen his skills for the bout. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: The Lost Boys (1987)
programme.desc: Two teenage brothers move with their mother to a coastal town in California, where the eldest falls in with a local biker gang. He soon discovers that his new friends are vampires and he is turning into one himself, forcing his younger brother to team up with a pair of heavily armed comic shop employees to battle the undead and save his soul. Comedy horror, starring Kiefer Sutherland, Jason Patric, Dianne Wiest, Corey Feldman and Corey Haim

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Jason Atherton and Andi Oliver and special guest Jessie Ware via the internet. Drinks expert Olly Smith also joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes. Plus, clips from the BBC food archive, featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Nigella Lawson

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: The theme this week is 'eat local' as Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond cook goat curry with chapatis. They are joined by farmer Jillian McEwan and actor and comedian Sanjeev Kohli, who selects a Scottish cider to accompany the food

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits properties in Preston and Liverpool in need of renovation, and Dion Dublin is in Nottingham to meet a couple with differing views on their project

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items at the International Antiques Fair of Wales in Builth Wells, Powys. Charlie Ross visits the National Fencing Musuem

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Catalans Dragons v Wakefield Trinity (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the sixth-round tie at John Smith's Stadium in Huddersfield. The Dragons won this competition for the first time in their history in 2018 and are expected to be in contention once again, while Trinity are looking to end a 57-year drought in the Challenge Cup. With commentary by Matt Newsum, and analysis by Jon Wilkin and Jamie Peacock

programme.title: Wreck-It Ralph (2012)
programme.desc: A video game villain grows tired of always being the bad guy, and ventures into other games in search of the chance to be a hero, unwittingly causing chaos in the process. When a deadly threat to the entire arcade appears, it falls to him to set things right. Animated adventure, with the voices of John C Reilly, Sarah Silverman and Jane Lynch

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a writers special edition of the general knowledge quiz. Contestants are Laurence Marks, Maurice Gran, Terry Deary, Eve Pollard, Jojo Moyes, Jeffery Deaver, Peter James and Dreda Say Mitchell

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this episode, the contestants include a pest controller, a slam poet and a train signaller

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob is furious to discover that Connie has cancelled the nurses' training in favour of the doctors, and at a board meeting, the tension between the pair continues to build. Overwhelmed with work and an ever-increasing workload, Noel makes a terrible mistake which leads to the death of a patient. Meanwhile, Loz stands up to Rory once and for all, freeing the team from Rory's control, and opinions in the ED are divided when an illegal stowaway falls from the sky. Starring Amanda Mealing and Charles Venn

programme.title: Gravity (2013)
programme.desc: Two astronauts are left stranded in orbit above the Earth when the debris from an exploding satellite destroys their shuttle. Left drifting in space with no way to contact mission control, they begin a perilous journey to reach the safety of a space station before their oxygen supply runs out. Thriller, starring Sandra Bullock and George Clooney

programme.title: Jason Manford's Muddle Class
programme.desc: The stand-up performer and actor presents a wealth of new material about growing up 'working class' then finding, over the years, that part of him has become 'middle class'

programme.title: Avengers: Age of Ultron (2015)
programme.desc: The superhero team faces a threat from within when Iron Man creates an artificial intelligence to take their place protecting the world. The android grows resentful of his creators, and decides his mission is best accomplished by wiping out the human race. Meanwhile, two vengeful superpowered twins seek to turn the Avengers against each other. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Hemsworth, Chris Evans and Scarlett Johansson

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble learn of the efforts of Longleat's keepers in the Australian bushfire animal rescue, and find out how to assess the health of an elephant from its poo

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Florist Lucy, personal trainer AJ and car sales executive Sophie compete, taking turns at doing one another's jobs before one of them walks away with the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Flower decides it is time to move because the Whiskers have been at the same burrow for five weeks. Her pregnancy makes her bad-tempered, which puts her three daughters Kinkajou, Daisy and Mozart on alert and at risk of eviction from the troop

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with a 1936 edition of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide, Michael Portillo ventures onto the European rail network, beginning an emotional rail journey which takes him deep into his family's past. In the golden city of Salamanca in northwestern Spain, Michael visits the University to hear of opposition to the fascist takeover of Spain by General Francisco Franco, and gains access to the General's archive of enemies of the state. Travelling onto the capital city of Madrid, Michael hears how the bombing of a small town in the Basque region in 1937 inspired one of the 20th century's most shocking works of art, before making his way to the town of Huesca. Michael's final stop is on the border with France at Canfranc station, where he learns of the role the old terminus played in the evacuation of Jews during the Second World War

programme.title: The Great British Year
programme.desc: The documentary follows wildlife throughout autumn, when the fading sun brings a change of energy to the landscape, while creatures prepare for shorter, darker days. Sika deer stags battle for supremacy, dormice curl up for their winter snooze, barnacle geese arrive from the Arctic and baby seals face the tempestuous waves, while salmon complete their epic journey to spawn before they die. By December, birds huddle together as the first snowflakes fall, and the seasonal cycle begins again. Narrated by Joseph Fiennes

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant has to use judgement and tactics to decide which other player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Steve Leonard and Christy Harrison for another test of wildlife knowledge, hosted by Kate Humble

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah learns from Leah that Regina is back in Inverness and heads over to her farm with Roy Brigg's rifle. George also visits his wife and she attempts to convince him that she is a reformed character giving him a letter revealing events that his family has kept from him. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode embarks on an alphabetical journey through dishes prepared by an array of TV chefs, beginning with recipes and ingredients linked by the letter A. Highlights include Jamie Oliver's savoury apple crumble, and Sophie Dahl's toffee apple crumble

programme.title: Incredible Medicine: Dr Weston's Casebook
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston continues to unlock the secrets of the human body, with cases including a girl with two hearts, a man who can sing two notes at once, a woman who can bend in amazing ways and a man who can run 350 miles without stopping

programme.title: World's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham presents the documentary from the team that produced Nature's Weirdest Events, exploring bizarre stories from across the globe. Here he unearths a whirlwind wedding ceremony, some exploding rocks and a woman with a twin inside her brain

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston upon Hull, Caroline Hawley uncovers a remarkable collection of suffragette postcards and David Harper is delighted by some Hull Daily Mail newspaper printing blocks, saved from the tip. Presented by Paul Martin

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Edmondson, Ed Gamble, Miquita Oliver and Kate Thornton test their knowledge in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Mozart is forced to seek support from the Whiskers clan or risk losing the pups. Meanwhile, Flower has her own new litter, and a well-timed rainstorm ensures there will be plenty of food for the meerkats. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Celebrity friends Chesney Hawkes and Debbie McGee scour the south east in search of all things antique, accompanied by experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson. Debbie hopes to conjure up a profit with a mantle clock and a hunting flask, while Chesney is taken by a 100-year-old weathervane

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley takes the contestants to her favourite dancefloor as they go backstage at Strictly Come Dancing. It's Movie Week, so they must each deliver two looks based on an allocated movie, while also embodying Strictly's brand of lashes, glamour and glitter. The best have the opportunity to work with make-up designer Lisa Armstrong on the live show. The next task sees the hopefuls transforming their models into a superhero, with the worst two challenged to produce a perfect glitter lip

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A selection of highlights from the stand-up show focusing on a different topic each week. First up are John Bishop, Sarah Millican, Phil Wang, Ellie Taylor, Ed Byrne, Angela Barnes, Gary Delaney, Tom Stade and Laura Lexx giving their thoughts on relationships

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: After advertising her home on Airbnb, Mandy takes in a guest from Russia - and they soon become more than friends. But can he be trusted? Comedy, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with Tony Way (After Life)

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Lola has not had a customer in her beauty salon for two days so Mandy suggests she introduce fish pedicures. Only to buy the wrong kind of fish. Sean Lock guest stars in the comedy, with Diane Morgan and Michelle Greenidge

programme.title: Semi-Detached
programme.desc: Lee Mack's put-upon anti-hero struggles his way through yet another farcical 30 minutes of his life in the real-life sitcom. Tonight, Stuart and his extended family take a ferry trip to scatter the ashes of a loved one at sea. However, when the hapless hero goes looking for his diarrhoea medication in his car, he discovers a stowaway, some class A drugs and some pretty explicit pornography prohibited by maritime law. Why does it always happen to him? Co-starring Patrick Baladi, Frances Barber, Clive Russell and Samantha Spiro

programme.title: Squeamish About…
programme.desc: Spoof documentary in which reporter Michael Squeamish (Matt Berry) takes a whistle-stop tour through the history of London, exploring everything from the Great Fire and the Blitz to jellied eels and Howard Westend, after whom the West End is named

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall

programme.title: Golf: Women's British Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights from the opening day at Royal Troon in South Ayrshire, where Hinako Shibuno of Japan was the reigning champion

programme.title: The Repair Shop: Fixing Britain
programme.desc: Jay Blades examines the evolution of modern-day transport through archive footage and revisiting three repairs from the barn - a Penny Farthing bike, a tram seat and a speedometer

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley joins a roller derby team ahead of a match, and Ashley John-Baptiste discovers how pushing yourself physically can lead to an increase in mental resilience

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble see how koalas get ready to mate and how to outwit the cunning hyena. Jean Johansson takes part in training on the lake to save a man overboard

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Head housekeeper Grace, telemarketing managing director Pierre and vineyard manager Adderley spend a day doing each other's jobs in a bid to prove theirs is the hardest

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Mozart is forced to seek support from the Whiskers clan or risk losing the pups. Meanwhile, Flower has her own new litter, and a well-timed rainstorm ensures there will be plenty of food for the meerkats. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Bears About the House
programme.desc: Part one of two. Conservationist Giles Clark embarks on his biggest mission to date - taking on the illegal wildlife trade and helping to build a new bear sanctuary in Laos, south-east Asia. Giles has been asked by good friend Matt Hunt, CEO of Free the Bears, to help for 12 months and is not long into his role when he has to step in to help Mary, a five-month-old sun bear. Mary was rescued after her mother was killed in the wild and was spotted on the back of a truck for sale into the illegal wildlife trade. Fragile and malnourished, she lives with Giles and Matt at home while she needs round-the-clock specialist care

programme.title: Ultimate Swarms
programme.desc: Zoologist and explorer George McGavin goes in search of some of the world's most impressive swarms to find out why these natural spectacles are a solution to surviving against the odds. He discovers that unlocking the secrets of how other animals swarm could be crucial to understanding an increasingly crowded human world

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Niall Strawson and Simon King for another test of wildlife knowledge, hosted by Kate Humble

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: As Sarah and George depart with Leah and David to Canberra, a distraught Regina attempts to stop George, warning of her inability to help him if he doesn't remain in Inverness. Post operative Douglas remains in hospital and Jack and Henry have a heated discussion about the ethicacy of informing him of his prognosis, with Elizabeth insisting on him being kept in ignorance

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: MasterChef's John Torode embarks on an alphabetical journey of dishes, with this episode's recipes linked by the letter P. The Hairy Bikers cook paella and Nigel Slater bakes plum pudding cake

programme.title: Incredible Medicine: Dr Weston's Casebook
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston continues to unlock the secrets of the human body, investigating some of the most extraordinary cases in medicine. First, she hears the story of a man with bones much stronger than the norm, and a girl whose arm will not stop growing. Dr Weston also learns of a woman who became pregnant with twins, but in two separate wombs, and a woman with a condition that means she is able to get lost within her own home

programme.title: World's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham presents another selection of bizarre facts and occurrences, including how deadly poisonous frogs can save lives and why people have a tendency to see faces in everything. Plus, the woman whose pants spontaneously burst into flame

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the City Hall in Kingston upon Hull, antiques experts Caroline Hawley and David Harper hunt for objects to take to auction. Caroline is entertained by the story behind some Laurel and Hardy autographs and David is gripped by a collection of postcards between young lovers from the Edwardian era. Presenter Paul Martin talks to playwright James Graham about the legacy of Hull's year as the UK's City of Culture

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ade Edmondson, Ed Gamble, Miquita Oliver and Kate Thornton go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Mozart is allowed to return to the family, while Flower and the pups get into a fight with the Commandos when the Whiskers are split up. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Highlights of the opening day of the Third Test, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton, as the teams looked to conclude the series on a high

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don makes plans for next spring by planting some early flowering wildflowers. He also gives his recipes for the different compost mixes he employs to take successful cuttings. Nick Bailey continues his quest for the South African origins of much-loved summer bulbs, while in the heart of London the programme meets a keen gardener who has filled her houseboat with ornamental and edible plants. Plus, a trip to Preston to visit a woman passionate about propagation and, in particular, pineapples

programme.title: Lady Bird (2017)
programme.desc: Golden Globe-winning comic drama, written and directed by Greta Gerwig. Artistic California teenager Lady Bird expects much from life. However, the strained relationship with her mother and failing family finances keep clipping her wings, while early romance leads to heartache. Saoirse Ronan, Laurie Metcalf and Tracy Letts star

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Louise and Tom decide they are too easily distracted by arguments that go nowhere, and try to adopt a new outlook. Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Katie Razzall presents analysis of the day's national and international events

programme.title: Golf: Women's British Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights from the second day at Royal Troon in South Ayrshire, where Hinako Shibuno of Japan was the reigning champion

programme.title: The Levelling (2016)
programme.desc: A trainee vet returns to her family farm after her brother's suicide and attempts to build bridges with her estranged father. Drama, starring Ellie Kendrick, David Troughton and Jack Holden

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Victoria Derbyshire investigates the impact of lockdown for those living with an abusive partner, revealing the scale of domestic violence at the height of the crisis and meeting some of those who managed to escape. She asks if the Government has done enough for those who were put in danger by having to stay at home

programme.title: Cuba: Castro vs the World
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary about Fidel Castro's leadership of Cuba, beginning with a look at his rise to power in 1959, which earned the hostility of the US and the support of the Soviet Union, a situation that culminated in the Cuban missile crisis. The programme also examines how Castro's government supported liberation struggles in other nations, including Algeria, Bolivia and Angola

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Unsuspecting parents are shipwrecked by daughter Elvie as their wedding day takes a very wet and windswept turn. However, being shipwrecked on a desert island is only the start of the wedding day surprises. After all, it can't be a real desert island without a treasure hunt can it?

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood investigate odd auditory occurrences, including a man who collects worms by grunting, and a gang of prairie dogs doing a Mexican wave

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends compete in the World Gurning Championships in Egremont

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Mark Wright and Lauren Layfield build an 'adventure den' for nine-year-old Rufus, who has leukaemia, and loves climbing around even though he cannot always join in with his friends

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall challenges a cheetah to a race and meets a cute but deadly bird

programme.title: Rise of the Continents
programme.desc: Geologist Professor Iain Stewart examines key moments in the development of the world's major land masses. He begins by looking at how Africa was formed from the wreckage of a long-lost supercontinent, with its landmarks, mineral wealth and wildlife providing clues that help piece together the story of its creation. He also reveals how this deep history has left its mark on modern day Africa and the world

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A millionaire tries to save jungle tigers on the Indonesian island of Sumatra by capturing them in their natural habitat, then transporting them to his own land and setting them free. However, zoologist Alan Rabinowitz is unsure about the wisdom of his scheme, particularly given the dangers the big cats pose to humans, and decides to investigate further

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary following life on farms in Scotland and the Lake District. In Armadale, a hill farmer prepares for one of Europe's biggest sheep sales, while in Haweswater, a couple take on the biggest gamble of their lives by moving to a larger farm 10 miles north. An Aberdeenshire couple who began farming four years ago plan to raise baby ostriches, and a Northumberland shepherd prepares for the National English Sheepdog trials, and new farmers hatch ostriches

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson reveals a secret passion when she prepares her dark and deviant liquorice pudding, then extols the virtues of fregola - the Sardinian sun-dried and toasted couscous - with tomato and clams. Her dinner-party guests are served butterflied leg of lamb and baby aubergines with oregano and roasted red onion, followed by meringue gelato cake with sharp raspberries and dark chocolate sauce for dessert

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers create classic puddings and explore the history and traditions behind Britain's favourite desserts, demonstrating recipes for a creamy chicken, ham and leek pie and the perfect apple pie

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: An enthusiastic Leona McCalla takes her husband and daughters Down Under, to see if they can share the love of Australia that she has developed on previous trips. Leona's hope is that they will agree to make the current temporary stay permanent. Nicki Chapman follows the clan's experiences

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner accompanies Johnny Ball on a trip to his childhood home of Bristol, where he recalls his love of maths challenges in the classroom and the impact of ration books on the supply of sweets at his local corner shop

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: The former Strictly Come Dancing judge takes ex-Homes Under the Hammer presenter Lucy Alexander back to Herne Bay in Kent to reminisce about a holiday she had in 1979

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores nature's evolutionary anomalies, beginning by examining how the unique features of the chameleon and the giraffe have benefited them in their respective environments. He then looks at curious creatures that have led to accusations of forgery, such as the duck-billed platypus and the midwife toad

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Professor Doug Emlen examines how animals use their bodies as weapons in epic battles to gore, stab, crush and batter their adversaries to death, from elephants to rhinoceros beetles, assessing whether they are more than just instruments of blunt force. For the professor this has been a lifetime's ambition to discover the secrets of nature's arms races, what triggers them and what they can teach us about the most formidable weapons on Earth - our own

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: This time, Michael Portillo ventures on a rail journey through Germany, guided by Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide published in 1936. Starting in the vibrant city of Berlin, Michael visits the Museum of Modern Art in Berlin's Kreuzberg, where he sees how a leading artist of the era, George Grosz, warned of the rise of fascism in a haunting self-portrait. In the Schöneberg district of the capital, Michael researches the decadent night scene of the 1920s, and looks at how cabaret culture is being revived today. When he reaches Weimar, Michael investigates the beginnings of Bauhaus design and visits the movement's first building, before travelling onto Stuttgart, where he takes a ride in a high-performance Porsche to the manufacturer's Stuttgart headquarters

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Highlights of the second day of the Third Test, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton. The hosts were victorious in a remarkable match at the start of the series, defying the odds on the final day to chase down their target, but they'll be hoping for a more consistent performance in this match against the youthful tourists

programme.title: Sean Connery: In His Own Words
programme.desc: Friends and actors pay tribute to Sean Connery, in a programme first shown in 2015 as the movie legend reached his 85th birthday. Dougray Scott, Robert Carlyle, Pierce Brosnan, Terry Gilliam and George Lucas are some of the big names who discuss Connery's iconic Bond movies, as well as many of his other films, including The Untouchables, for which he won the Best Supporting Actor Oscar

programme.title: Entrapment (1999)
programme.desc: An elusive professional art thief suspected of stealing a priceless painting is pursued by an insurance agent. However, instead of bringing the criminal to book, she teams up with him to plot the robbery of a lifetime - although neither is sure the other can be trusted. Crime thriller, starring Sean Connery, Catherine Zeta-Jones, Ving Rhames, Will Patton and Maury Chaykin

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: A red-hot selection of summer sounds, featuring songs by John Travolta and Olivia Newton-John, the Undertones, Shaggy, Bananarama, Bobby Goldsboro, Bay City Rollers, Fun Boy Three, the Style Council and Don Henley. Other archive sizzlers include DJ Jazzy Jeff and the Fresh Prince, the Sundays, Sabrina, Chris Rea, the Barracudas, Zoe, Martha and the Muffins, Bryan Adams, Girls Aloud and ELO

programme.title: Golf: Women's British Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights from the third day at Royal Troon in South Ayrshire. Hinako Shibuno of Japan moved into the lead at this stage last year in Milton Keynes and went on to finish one stroke clear of America's Lizette Salas to claim the title

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The gravedigger and amateur cameraman heads off to see the salmon jumping and films the round-up of the Exmoor ponies, one of England's rarest breeds. The annual event gets lively as the herd's owners, the Milton family, try to separate the mares and foals from the stallions

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a faraway mountain nestled in misty clouds and foothills, creating the scene using soft, brilliant colours

programme.title: Joanna Lumley in the Land of the Northern Lights
programme.desc: The actress travels up through Norway to Svalbard, the most northerly permanently inhabited place on Earth, where she experiences temperatures approaching minus 30C. Her aim is to fulfil a lifetime ambition to see the natural phenomenon, the Northern Lights. Along the way she explores the fjords of Lofoten, learns to ride a snowmobile and spends a night in a hotel made of ice

programme.title: Quartet (2012)
programme.desc: Three former opera singers prepare for their annual concert to mark Verdi's birthday and raise funds for their retirement home. However, this year, a famous diva has joined the group, and her history with the others threatens the performance. Comedy drama, directed by Dustin Hoffman and starring Maggie Smith, Billy Connolly, Tom Courtenay, Pauline Collins and Michael Gambon

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at TV appearances made over the years by multi-award-winning actress Maggie Smith, capturing the milestones and highlights of her life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Afua Hirsch explores three African countries through their art, music and culture, beginning by charting Ethiopia's 3,000-year history

programme.title: Handmade in Africa
programme.desc: Documentary exploring inherited knowledge and the meaning of tradition, craftsmanship and artistic processes in three African cultures, beginning with the weaving of a bamboo house in the remote highlands of southern Ethiopia by members of the Dorze minority ethnic group

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: Dr James Fox takes viewers on a tour of some of the most profound artworks from Tate Britain's collection, including a self-portrait by 18th-century artist William Hogarth

programme.title: Museums in Quarantine
programme.desc: Art historian Janina Ramirez takes a virtual tour of the British Museum, whose galleries she has paced since she was a girl. Here she highlights objects as varied as an Aztec decorated skull, ancient Egyptian cat mummies and an 18th-century tea set

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: Dr Adam Rutherford tells the story of how Dr Henry Gray and Dr Henry Carter produced academic textbook Gray's Anatomy - the world's most famous study of the human body. Based on dissections they performed themselves, the accuracy of its descriptions and the stark beauty of its illustrations made it an instant bestseller

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints a faraway mountain nestled in misty clouds and foothills, creating the scene using soft, brilliant colours

programme.title: Joanna Lumley in the Land of the Northern Lights
programme.desc: The actress travels up through Norway to Svalbard, the most northerly permanently inhabited place on Earth, where she experiences temperatures approaching minus 30C. Her aim is to fulfil a lifetime ambition to see the natural phenomenon, the Northern Lights. Along the way she explores the fjords of Lofoten, learns to ride a snowmobile and spends a night in a hotel made of ice

programme.title: Easy Listening Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances by some of the best-known easy-listening musicians of the 1960s and 70s, including Sergio Mendes & Brasil 66, Burt Bacharach, the Carpenters and Andy Williams

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A musical compilation, featuring mature rockers Tina Turner and Phil Collins, all-conquering funki dredds Soul II Soul, dance queen Paula Abdul, northern soul girl Lisa Stansfield and the stubborn indie pop of the Beautiful South. Madchester favourites Stone Roses and Happy Mondays put in an appearance, along with Stock Aitken Waterman stalwarts Kylie Minogue, Jason Donovan and Sonia. Plus, Sydney Youngblood, Mike and the Mechanics, Rebel MC and Double Trouble, along with duets from strange bedfellows Marc Almond and Gene Pitney and Linda Ronstadt and Aaron Neville

programme.title: Shirley Bassey at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of archive performances by the singer first shown to mark her 76th birthday - some 60 years after her vocal talent first achieved public recognition. Featuring footage from Show of the Week, The Shirley Bassey Show, the 2007 Glastonbury Festival and the star's Electric Proms show in 2009. Songs include Goldfinger, Big Spender and Diamonds are Forever

programme.title: Dame Shirley Bassey: Electric Proms 2009
programme.desc: Trevor Nelson and Edith Bowman present highlights of the singer's performance to celebrate her 50 years in showbusiness. Classic tracks such as Big Spender and Goldfinger appear alongside songs from her album The Performance, with accompaniment by the BBC Concert Orchestra and guest collaborators David Arnold, James Dean Bradfield and Richard Hawley

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob celebrates Shirley Bassey's 2009 album The Performance, the David Arnold-produced record that was her first studio release in two decades, by examining a selection of her greatest archive performances. He also meets songwriters who contributed to the album, including Gary Barlow, Pet Shop Boys, Manic Street Preachers, KT Tunstall and frequent James Bond composer John Barry

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: From 2019, Grammy winner Angelique Kidjo makes her Proms debut at the Royal Albert Hall with her nine-piece band in a tribute to salsa star Celia Cruz. Clara Amfo explores the links between the music of Cuban diva, Cruz and the traditional Yoruba music that came to America during the slave trade, and how Kidjo has combined these influences with the West-African Afrobeat and juju styles

programme.title: The Defiant Ones
programme.desc: Jimmy Iovine talks about his worry that that music bubble might burst due the emergence of Napster. Although he was on a roll with Dr Dre and an enviable list of hot new acts, he also looked to forge partnerships and opportunities to secure their future. An opportune sit-down between Jimmy and Dre led to the idea of Beats Electronics, the speaker-headphone phenomenon that would vault the partners to new heights and pave the way for an historic 2014 deal with Apple Music

programme.title: Flat Pack Pop: Sweden's Music Miracle
programme.desc: James Ballardie charts Sweden's rise as a musical superpower, exploring the unique songwriting formula that inspired some of the biggest hits of the last 30 years. What started out as an experiment on the Stockholm underground club scene in the 1990s blossomed into a genre of its own, spearheaded by the likes of music producer and songwriter Denniz Pop. These unlikely heroes of bubblegum pop launched the careers of Britney Spears, the Backstreet Boys, Westlife and others. Featuring interviews with producers and artists, including Katy Perry, Justin Timberlake, Ace of Base and Robyn

programme.title: Shirley Bassey at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of archive performances by the singer first shown to mark her 76th birthday - some 60 years after her vocal talent first achieved public recognition. Featuring footage from Show of the Week, The Shirley Bassey Show, the 2007 Glastonbury Festival and the star's Electric Proms show in 2009. Songs include Goldfinger, Big Spender and Diamonds are Forever

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Central Scotland, Bobby and Anne must gather their pregnant ewes from the hills ready for lambing, while east of Inverness, Mel and Martin move into a caravan on site to deal with the imminent arrival of hundreds of lambs. On the Isle of Lewis, Sandy makes tweed curtains for the holiday let as Easter approaches, and an unexpected tragedy hits the Lennoxes

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin's epic expedition from the North Pole to the South one. In the first leg of his journey, he leaves the Arctic and travel by a variety of vehicles through Russia, Norway and Finland. He interviews Father Christmas, takes a sauna with a Finnish MP and nearly starts a new life in a Norwegian lighthouse along the way

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The murderer launches a twisted plan to highlight cutbacks in mental health care - by dispatching schizophrenic people to commit synchronised violent crimes in Malmo and Copenhagen. Saga and Martin hope a teenage runaway could prove to be the witness who gives them a vital break in the case. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Several police officers are found innocent of beating an immigrant to death, but one of them is kidnapped. He is later found by the victim's brother - who then faces an agonising choice over whether to forgive him or exact revenge. Meanwhile, Saga makes contact with the killer for the first time. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The murderer holds a bus full of schoolchildren hostage, and vows he will only let his captives go if members of the public set fire to the premises of five companies that indirectly benefit from child labour. Meanwhile, Saga becomes convinced the culprit is one of her fellow police officers. Crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: A witness statement leads Saga and Martin to arrest one of their colleagues on suspicion of masterminding the crime spree, but he appears to have an alibi for all the incidents. Meanwhile, the murderer announces he has achieved his goals, and declares his reign of terror has come to an end - though Saga is certain his campaign is far from over. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: Avicii: True Stories (2017)
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the Swedish DJ, who died in 2018, featuring extensive personal and family archive and behind-the-scenes footage to paint a picture of his life

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Documentary examining Peter Sutcliffe's crimes and asking whether 1970s attitudes to women influenced the investigation, leaving the murderer free to kill again

programme.title: The Zoo

programme.desc: It is the day of the zoo's annual stocktake, but as all the animals get ready to be counted, a group of tiny frogs takes the opportunity to escape to Paris

programme.desc: The baboons upset the entire zoo with their constant yelling and arguing and pressure mounts on baboon keeper Kate to keep her pesky primates happy and quiet

programme.desc: The keepers race against the clock to move the flamingos into a new enclosure

programme.desc: Louis Dunn is in his home town of Manchester to meet Idris, a young amputee footballer with big dreams. Idris has his eye on a more competitive future, and is hoping to make it to the final training session for the England Amputee team before the World Cup in Mexico

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: A special episode about the life of Mary, Queen of Scots, following her as she stumbles from young girl in Scotland to queen in France, back to queen in Scotland, and finally to Elizabeth I's arch enemy. Plus, the programme meets silver-nosed Danish scientist Tycho Brahe, and Mr H gives his unique take on the epic Battle of Lepanto in another edition of History's Craziest Fools

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: On a trip to Japan, Penfold's ill-timed comfort break sparks a world-wide porcelain revolution led by the evil super-intelligent toilet Dr Loocifer. Sick of being sat on, every lavatory on the planet downs lids and marches to Dr Loocifer's tune. After an exciting chase through the streets, rooftops and train tops of Tokyo, Danger Mouse and Penfold infiltrate the evil toilet's lair and overcome a mighty monster toilet to bring a huge sigh of relief to everyone of earth

programme.title: Diddy TV
programme.desc: Aunty meets her match in the Kiss Chase

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Mr Malone holds a mock trial to resolve a dispute Martha and Ollie are having over the ownership of Edison, a cuddly lightbulb they won together at a fairground hoopla. Rufus is appointed Ollie’s ineffective lawyer. (His amenability is something his girlfriend Kat finds very frustrating). Lily is Martha’s disappointing lawyer - whilst promising an epic opening statement, Lily makes it all about Rob being in America. Ollie asks Martha to prove she can throw a hoop on target. Martha objects, but Judge Kat insists that it will be amusing to witness Martha throw hoops. Martha applies the scientific calculations she gave to Ollie to help him win Edison, but she still fails to throw a hoop successfully. Ollie nears victory when the court adjourns because Mr Malone is hungry. Later, whilst Rufus admits to Kat that he doesn’t feel he is cut out to be a lawyer, he offers Kat the largest slice of cake even though he wants it. This infuriates Kat. She tells Rufus that he needs to be less easy-goi

As they journey deeper into the forest, past their favourite swimming hole, the kids make a series of bewildering discoveries and eventually realise they're completely and utterly lost. With one of the gang's future ambitions at stake, they face a race against time to get back to the stables. programme.desc: Andy Day goes on another prehistoric adventure after an accident at the museum ruins his new prehistoric exhibit. Arriving 150 million years in the past he tracks down the peculiar camptosaurus and plays hide-and-seek with a fierce allosaurus

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bitz and Bob's junk stall sells out of everything, including Bob's robo-helmet! Bob is horrified

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters get shrunk by Glitch as he does some alarming farming at the Academy

programme.title: YolanDa's Band Jam
programme.desc: YolanDa kicks off the show with 'Bass Face', a song all about pitch, and the guests are tuba stars Theon Cross and Oren Marshall. They bring a traditional tuba alongside a customised one, ‘The Orenophone'. In the Fact Jam, we find out how long a tuba would be when stretched out, and that the biggest tuba in the world is three times the size of a normal one. A short film features two football-loving children as they write a football-themed song with the help of Nigel Clark. A funky song starring the tuba called 'Dinosaur Dance' follows

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle tries to impress on his first day as a listening dog at school

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Lady has run out of wool, so Sarah and Duck help her to harvest the weeping woollen tree

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: When magical toy Pepi Nana wakes up in the night, she writes a letter to the moon

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: A meteor shower of gemstones falls on the planet. Baby Soup Dragon beats Tiny and Small in a game to win them. The others continue the game without him and his stones, he realises that there is more fun to be had in playing than just winning

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Igglepiggle goes for a long journey and gets lost. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Rob Delaney reads Ten in the Bed, by Penny Dale

programme.title: Show Me Show Me
programme.desc: Miss Mouse is a scurrying lizard, and Chris Jarvis and Pui Fan Lee discover precious fossils that are even older than Chris' old socks

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos watch some children untie balloons, which fly up high. Great BigHoo unties all the balloons and he, Toodloo, Chickedy and Chick fly up and up into the sky

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa has fun watching Dipsy stretch up, like a very tall tower. The Tubby Toaster goes crazy and fires out lots of Tubby Toast which ends up in a tall tower too. The Teletubbies watch children climb the stairs to the top of a tall tower in Tummy Tales

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: Topsy gets the musical stones ready for a noisy game, then leaves Raa Raa in charge to make sure no one jumps on them before it begins

programme.title: Postman Pat: SDS
programme.desc: The mailman tries to deliver an enormous tree that Alf has ordered for Thompson Ground - but Pat finds it very difficult to transport and must do his best to drive slowly

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny finds a frog in the garden and wants to keep her, so he makes her a new home

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny tries to play his car park game with Flop, but Charlie is visiting and keeps messing up the game

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels are digging in the garden when they come across a fossil, so Duggee and Narrator explain all about fossils and prehistoric times. When the Squirrels go outside, it seems like they've gone right back in time

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The dog is using his trusty binoculars to do some birdwatching and the squirrels want to have a go too, but Roly finds being very quiet is difficult

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: It's an aerial adventure in Gibraltar as the team follows a group of migrating baby birds. Cartoon following four superheroes as they explore the world

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Benjamin becomes the unwitting passenger of a flyaway kite - so Peter and Lily put aside their differences to rescue their friend

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: While exploring a dark and spooky cave, the Octonauts discover the fossil of a powerful, prehistoric fish, but it may be more than just a fossil

programme.title: Biggleton

programme.desc: Mr Tumble makes breakfast for Tumble Ted. Meanwhile, Justin and his friends are collecting eggs for their breakfast

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Justin decides to dig up a dinosaur and turn his house into a museum

programme.desc: Andy Day reveals the different ways in which animals sleep, revealing how many young apes must learn how to make a bed out of leaves. In Tibet, bar-headed geese make a soft fluffy nest out of feathers on the hard rock, while Meerkats spend the night in burrows underground

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

programme.desc: The twins cannot sleep in their bunk beds because their room is being decorated by DIY Derek, so Dad tells them they are going on a camping adventure

programme.desc: Some things are easy to agree on, like snow is cold and the sun is hot. But Hugo finds it harder to agree on other things, like when he wants to play basketball but his friends want to play dolls! They learn to compromise, and go on a fun bumper car ride all together!

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: Rory visits Jess and her sheep dog Mo, and watches the pair as they herd sheep, before having a go himself. He also helps clean out Mo's kennel and ensure she has food and water

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: When Big Fish can't go on a holibob with the Olobobs, they bring their holiday to her!

programme.desc: Rory visits Sebastian and his guinea pig Bonbon who has a lump on her leg that needs checking by a vet. Ferne goes to meet a vet who is caring for a badger at a wildlife hospital

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Kari is scared of playing her trumpet in front of the Treasure Champs, but Barry reminds her how he had courage to buy her trumpet in the first place. Barry also shows courage by going into a dark cave where a funny creature lives. Storyteller Ben Langley visits a zoo to tell the story of Daniel and the Lion's den

programme.desc: A fable about how frogs lost the ability to sing after jealous birds played a trick on them, reducing their voices to nothing more than a croak

programme.desc: Andy jets off in his safari-mobile to Brazil to take photos of a family of marmosets

programme.desc: When Bob mixes up a delivery of cakes with a delivery of fish, Mrs Juniper is forced to sell the fish. James and the kids help Mrs Juniper turn her cafe into a fish shop

programme.title: Sarah & Duck

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Charlie practises doing magic tricks, but poor Lola cannot get the hang of it

programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.desc: Small is bored, and with Tiny, Major and Mother all too busy, he asks Granny to play with him. He is doubtful - but he couldn't have been more mistaken as she takes him on an adventure to a place he has never seen before

programme.desc: Makka Pakka cannot find any stones in the garden, so his friends help him. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.desc: An Axminster property that took four years to develop, a small office in Basingstoke crying out to be a house and a Birkenhead terrace that has three - or maybe four - bedrooms

While they're busy, presenter Charlie Ross pays a visit to a recording studio in Wales, Rockfields

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine's self-doubt is addressed by an actor, and a missed deadline puts Kam under pressure
