programme.desc: Katharine Merry, Derek Redmond, Ryan Sidebottom, Paul Robinson and Peter Shilton form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes property shopping in Exmoor with a newlywed couple who have a £625k budget for a home that will house his parents and provide her with land for horses

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Head housekeeper Grace, telemarketing managing director Pierre and vineyard manager Adderley spend a day doing each other's jobs before judging whose is the hardest

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth tour the West Country, where Roo meets Exmoor's equine inhabitants and Angus bares all at an exclusive beach that can only be accessed by tunnel

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: Iolo Williams continues his exploration of the Cambrian mountains, heading to the Tywi forest to look for goshawks. He tracks these birds of prey during the late part of the nesting season and finds fully grown chicks in a huge nest with bits of eaten prey below. Iolo also explores the Elan Valley to find the curlew, one of Wales' most critically endangered bird species, as they attempt to breed on rough ground near the reservoirs. And he discovers a stunning day-flying moth called the Welsh clearwing

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering some of the most entertaining team challenges - and how different dynamics can lead to either success or chaos in the kitchen. They also revisit a 2015 semi-final task which saw Sheree Murphy, Scott Maslen, Rylan Clark-Neal, Sam Nixon, Chesney Hawkes, Mica Paris, Kimberly Wyatt and Tom Parker thrown together in a Victorian-style village, using only ingredients widely available during that era

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and Martha's romantic weekend on a remote island is cut short when the hotel owner is stabbed to death in his room in what appears to be an attempted robbery. But since the flower beds outside the window are undisturbed, Humphrey is certain that one of the victim's associates played a part in his death. There's just one problem - since the DI had a direct view of the only staircase leading up to the room, he is the alibi for all the suspects. Meanwhile, JP worries about his marriage and seeks relationship advice from Dwayne

programme.title: Can Sex Offenders Change?
programme.desc: Becky Southworth, the daughter of a convicted sex offender, investigates the world of sex offender rehabilitation. Filming across the UK in anonymous locations, she talks to a number of men living in the community, asking what drove them to their horrific crimes, and finding out what treatment is available to stop them reoffending

programme.title: Full House
programme.desc: A slice of life at Judges bingo hall in Tonypandy, as families, couples and groups of friends are poised with their dabbers ready, hoping to land themselves the jackpot. But for club regulars it is not all about the winning. It's also a chance to get together and sort their lives out. Cameras eavesdrop on the likes of Debbie and her bingo-hating partner Eric, cash-strapped single mum Rose and her best friend Sophie, and tattoo-shop owner AJ, who has something he wants to share with his daughter and business partner Tammy

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle find out what happens when a silverback gorilla has his authority threatened and meet a new pancake tortoise. Jean Johansson falls for a fennec fox who's looking for a partner

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The team explores a beautiful period cottage in Seend in Wiltshire, a semi in Neath in south Wales and a fire-damaged property in Wolverhampton, which have been sold at auction

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items at Liverpool's St George's Hall, while presenter Eric Knowles finds out why the building came to be built in the city

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Rose shows Kam there is a world beyond school. Meanwhile, it seems Ash may have reached the end of the road with Helena

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team from the world of arts and entertainment comprising actor Simon Greenall, singer Toyah Willcox, arts correspondent Nick Higham, award-winning saxophonist YolanDa Brown and Proms presenter Katie Derham set out to triumph over the general knowledge Goliaths and win the cash prize for charity. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the East Midlands to help a couple find a comfortable country home that brings them closer to family for £450,000. While in the region, Jonnie visits the Leicester Line of the Grand Union canal, a stretch where narrow boats have to navigate a total of 59 locks. He tries his hand at lock keeper's duties, including operating the floodgates

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Climbing guide Jack, master tea-taster Sebastian and luxury couture retailer Onita go head to head in a bid to prove their job is the hardest and claim a £1,000 prize. Day one is steep challenge for Onita as she finds out how many times she's expected to climb one of London's most iconic tourist attractions. The next day sees Jack pushed for time as he grapples with the complexity of grading and blending tea leaves. On the final day, Jack and Sebastian try to master the science of window dressing and selling high-end women's clothing in a luxury boutique

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine enjoys the fun of the fair, while Angus Ashworth heads to Plymouth to discover the turbulent history of gin, before having a go as a Master Distiller. They then join up at their final auction in Clevedon, north Somerset

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones is joined by co-host Iain Stirling to round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Documentary looking at how the quiz show has evolved since it was first shown in January 1970 to remain successful in the present day. Archive footage includes when 19 million people tuned in to see the Princess Royal line-up alongside captain Emlyn Hughes, and guests relive their memories of appearing alongside past hosts David Vine and David Coleman, as well as current incumbent Sue Barker. Among the many contributors are Sebastian Coe, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Alan Shearer, Denise Lewis, Gary Lineker, Gabby Logan and current captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen is on a quest for cash, as he reviews the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. It's a story of one-armed bandits, potato rustlers and £30 pints, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Bob Mortimer, Gabby Logan, Katherine Parkinson and Steve Backshall to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian tries out being a teacher at Monnow Primary School in Bettws, Gwent. However, 'Mr Gilbert' soon feels the pressure as he tries to impress his colleagues and the children in his class

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: There's a commotion in the housing estate, and Conor and Jock discover can-trailers - tearaways from other estates with open paint cans tied around bikes intent on destroying the rival estate. The lads stop their rivals, but the encounter leads to an argument and Jock is challenged to a boxing match - which he reluctantly agrees to. The duo soon discover his prospective opponent is a champion fighter, meaning Jock has to embark on an extensive training programme in order to sharpen his skills for the bout. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: The Lost Boys (1987)
programme.desc: Two teenage brothers move with their mother to a coastal town in California, where the eldest falls in with a local biker gang. He soon discovers that his new friends are vampires and he is turning into one himself, forcing his younger brother to team up with a pair of heavily armed comic shop employees to battle the undead and save his soul. Comedy horror, starring Kiefer Sutherland, Jason Patric, Dianne Wiest, Corey Feldman and Corey Haim

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Jason Atherton and Andi Oliver and special guest Jessie Ware via the internet. Drinks expert Olly Smith also joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes. Plus, clips from the BBC food archive, featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Nigella Lawson

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: The theme this week is 'eat local' as Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond cook goat curry with chapatis. They are joined by farmer Jillian McEwan and actor and comedian Sanjeev Kohli, who selects a Scottish cider to accompany the food

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits properties in Preston and Liverpool in need of renovation, and Dion Dublin is in Nottingham to meet a couple with differing views on their project

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items at the International Antiques Fair of Wales in Builth Wells, Powys. Charlie Ross visits the National Fencing Musuem

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Catalans Dragons v Wakefield Trinity (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the sixth-round tie at John Smith's Stadium in Huddersfield. The Dragons won this competition for the first time in their history in 2018 and are expected to be in contention once again, while Trinity are looking to end a 57-year drought in the Challenge Cup. With commentary by Matt Newsum, and analysis by Jon Wilkin and Jamie Peacock

programme.title: Wreck-It Ralph (2012)
programme.desc: A video game villain grows tired of always being the bad guy, and ventures into other games in search of the chance to be a hero, unwittingly causing chaos in the process. When a deadly threat to the entire arcade appears, it falls to him to set things right. Animated adventure, with the voices of John C Reilly, Sarah Silverman and Jane Lynch

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a writers special edition of the general knowledge quiz. Contestants are Laurence Marks, Maurice Gran, Terry Deary, Eve Pollard, Jojo Moyes, Jeffery Deaver, Peter James and Dreda Say Mitchell

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this episode, the contestants include a pest controller, a slam poet and a train signaller

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob is furious to discover that Connie has cancelled the nurses' training in favour of the doctors, and at a board meeting, the tension between the pair continues to build. Overwhelmed with work and an ever-increasing workload, Noel makes a terrible mistake which leads to the death of a patient. Meanwhile, Loz stands up to Rory once and for all, freeing the team from Rory's control, and opinions in the ED are divided when an illegal stowaway falls from the sky. Starring Amanda Mealing and Charles Venn

programme.title: Gravity (2013)
programme.desc: Two astronauts are left stranded in orbit above the Earth when the debris from an exploding satellite destroys their shuttle. Left drifting in space with no way to contact mission control, they begin a perilous journey to reach the safety of a space station before their oxygen supply runs out. Thriller, starring Sandra Bullock and George Clooney

programme.title: Jason Manford's Muddle Class
programme.desc: The stand-up performer and actor presents a wealth of new material about growing up 'working class' then finding, over the years, that part of him has become 'middle class'

programme.title: Avengers: Age of Ultron (2015)
programme.desc: The superhero team faces a threat from within when Iron Man creates an artificial intelligence to take their place protecting the world. The android grows resentful of his creators, and decides his mission is best accomplished by wiping out the human race. Meanwhile, two vengeful superpowered twins seek to turn the Avengers against each other. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Hemsworth, Chris Evans and Scarlett Johansson

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley with a report on the counterfeit contact lenses that can make the wearer's eyesight worse, and the fake waste disposal companies that could cost consumers a fortune. Michelle also shows how to identify fake emails and dangerous links online, and reveals how criminals can even embed fake messages into a legitimate text chain on a phone

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering some of the most entertaining team challenges - and how different dynamics can lead to either success or chaos in the kitchen. They also revisit a 2015 semi-final task which saw Sheree Murphy, Scott Maslen, Rylan Clark-Neal, Sam Nixon, Chesney Hawkes, Mica Paris, Kimberly Wyatt and Tom Parker thrown together in a Victorian-style village, using only ingredients widely available during that era

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and Martha's romantic weekend on a remote island is cut short when the hotel owner is stabbed to death in his room in what appears to be an attempted robbery. But since the flower beds outside the window are undisturbed, Humphrey is certain that one of the victim's associates played a part in his death. There's just one problem - since the DI had a direct view of the only staircase leading up to the room, he is the alibi for all the suspects. Meanwhile, JP worries about his marriage and seeks relationship advice from Dwayne

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Highlights of recent events

programme.title: Can Sex Offenders Change?
programme.desc: Becky Southworth, the daughter of a convicted sex offender, investigates the world of sex offender rehabilitation. Filming across the UK in anonymous locations, she talks to a number of men living in the community, asking what drove them to their horrific crimes, and finding out what treatment is available to stop them reoffending

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police mount urgent searches for two people with dementia. Whilst out shopping with his wife, 83-year-old John turned his back for a moment, before realising that his 82-year-old wife Sheila has vanished on the busy streets of Gloucester town centre. After a frantic search around town, John calls Gloucestershire Police and a major search operation swings into action. Elsewhere in rural Gloucestershire, police scour lanes and wooded areas as they search for 79-year-old John, who disappeared from the home he shares with his wife, Marian when she was out visiting a family member for lunch

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle find out what happens when a silverback gorilla has his authority threatened and meet a new pancake tortoise. Jean Johansson falls for a fennec fox who's looking for a partner

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The team explores a beautiful period cottage in Seend in Wiltshire, a semi in Neath in south Wales and a fire-damaged property in Wolverhampton, which have been sold at auction

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dom helps track down fugitives with a bounty on their heads as he works as a private investigator in Waco, Texas. His mentor is James Fullner, a private investigator who's spent 15 years in the business, exposing cheating partners and business fraud, and apprehending fugitives on the run. Working alongside an armed team of investigators, on behalf of a local bail bondsman, they hit the streets in pursuit of a violent fugitive, a runaway drug addict and a wanted sex offender

programme.title: Politics Scotland
programme.desc: A round-up of political news and events at Westminster and Holyrood.

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Rose shows Kam there is a world beyond school. Meanwhile, it seems Ash may have reached the end of the road with Helena

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team from the world of arts and entertainment comprising actor Simon Greenall, singer Toyah Willcox, arts correspondent Nick Higham, award-winning saxophonist YolanDa Brown and Proms presenter Katie Derham set out to triumph over the general knowledge Goliaths and win the cash prize for charity. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the East Midlands to help a couple find a comfortable country home that brings them closer to family for £450,000. While in the region, Jonnie visits the Leicester Line of the Grand Union canal, a stretch where narrow boats have to navigate a total of 59 locks. He tries his hand at lock keeper's duties, including operating the floodgates

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Climbing guide Jack, master tea-taster Sebastian and luxury couture retailer Onita go head to head in a bid to prove their job is the hardest and claim a £1,000 prize. Day one is steep challenge for Onita as she finds out how many times she's expected to climb one of London's most iconic tourist attractions. The next day sees Jack pushed for time as he grapples with the complexity of grading and blending tea leaves. On the final day, Jack and Sebastian try to master the science of window dressing and selling high-end women's clothing in a luxury boutique

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine enjoys the fun of the fair, while Angus Ashworth heads to Plymouth to discover the turbulent history of gin, before having a go as a Master Distiller. They then join up at their final auction in Clevedon, north Somerset

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones is joined by co-host Iain Stirling to round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: A fed up Miranda makes a huge decision - she packs her bags to go travelling and this time it's for good. Stevie is devastated, while Penny is too wrapped up in renewing her wedding vows to notice. It seems only Gary can stop her going - but how does he really feel? Miranda Hart, Patricia Hodge, Tom Ellis and Sarah Hadland star in the hit comedy

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Documentary looking at how the quiz show has evolved since it was first shown in January 1970 to remain successful in the present day. Archive footage includes when 19 million people tuned in to see the Princess Royal line-up alongside captain Emlyn Hughes, and guests relive their memories of appearing alongside past hosts David Vine and David Coleman, as well as current incumbent Sue Barker. Among the many contributors are Sebastian Coe, Jessica Ennis-Hill, Alan Shearer, Denise Lewis, Gary Lineker, Gabby Logan and current captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Bob Mortimer, Gabby Logan, Katherine Parkinson and Steve Backshall to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: There's a commotion in the housing estate, and Conor and Jock discover can-trailers - tearaways from other estates with open paint cans tied around bikes intent on destroying the rival estate. The lads stop their rivals, but the encounter leads to an argument and Jock is challenged to a boxing match - which he reluctantly agrees to. The duo soon discover his prospective opponent is a champion fighter, meaning Jock has to embark on an extensive training programme in order to sharpen his skills for the bout. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: The Lost Boys (1987)
programme.desc: Two teenage brothers move with their mother to a coastal town in California, where the eldest falls in with a local biker gang. He soon discovers that his new friends are vampires and he is turning into one himself, forcing his younger brother to team up with a pair of heavily armed comic shop employees to battle the undead and save his soul. Comedy horror, starring Kiefer Sutherland, Jason Patric, Dianne Wiest, Corey Feldman and Corey Haim

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Jason Atherton and Andi Oliver and special guest Jessie Ware via the internet. Drinks expert Olly Smith also joins via video link to pick the wines to go with the studio dishes. Plus, clips from the BBC food archive, featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Nigella Lawson

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: The theme this week is 'eat local' as Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond cook goat curry with chapatis. They are joined by farmer Jillian McEwan and actor and comedian Sanjeev Kohli, who selects a Scottish cider to accompany the food

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits properties in Preston and Liverpool in need of renovation, and Dion Dublin is in Nottingham to meet a couple with differing views on their project

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams look for items at the International Antiques Fair of Wales in Builth Wells, Powys. Charlie Ross visits the National Fencing Musuem

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Catalans Dragons v Wakefield Trinity (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the sixth-round tie at John Smith's Stadium in Huddersfield. The Dragons won this competition for the first time in their history in 2018 and are expected to be in contention once again, while Trinity are looking to end a 57-year drought in the Challenge Cup. With commentary by Matt Newsum, and analysis by Jon Wilkin and Jamie Peacock

programme.title: Wreck-It Ralph (2012)
programme.desc: A video game villain grows tired of always being the bad guy, and ventures into other games in search of the chance to be a hero, unwittingly causing chaos in the process. When a deadly threat to the entire arcade appears, it falls to him to set things right. Animated adventure, with the voices of John C Reilly, Sarah Silverman and Jane Lynch

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a writers special edition of the general knowledge quiz. Contestants are Laurence Marks, Maurice Gran, Terry Deary, Eve Pollard, Jojo Moyes, Jeffery Deaver, Peter James and Dreda Say Mitchell

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this episode, the contestants include a pest controller, a slam poet and a train signaller

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jacob is furious to discover that Connie has cancelled the nurses' training in favour of the doctors, and at a board meeting, the tension between the pair continues to build. Overwhelmed with work and an ever-increasing workload, Noel makes a terrible mistake which leads to the death of a patient. Meanwhile, Loz stands up to Rory once and for all, freeing the team from Rory's control, and opinions in the ED are divided when an illegal stowaway falls from the sky. Starring Amanda Mealing and Charles Venn

programme.title: Gravity (2013)
programme.desc: Two astronauts are left stranded in orbit above the Earth when the debris from an exploding satellite destroys their shuttle. Left drifting in space with no way to contact mission control, they begin a perilous journey to reach the safety of a space station before their oxygen supply runs out. Thriller, starring Sandra Bullock and George Clooney

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Scottish Premiership matches, including Dundee United v Celtic, Rangers v Kilmarnock, Aberdeen v Livingston and St Mirren v Ross County. Jonathan Sutherland is joined by Shaun Maloney and Leanne Crichton for reaction

programme.title: Jason Manford's Muddle Class
programme.desc: The stand-up performer and actor presents a wealth of new material about growing up 'working class' then finding, over the years, that part of him has become 'middle class'

programme.title: Avengers: Age of Ultron (2015)
programme.desc: The superhero team faces a threat from within when Iron Man creates an artificial intelligence to take their place protecting the world. The android grows resentful of his creators, and decides his mission is best accomplished by wiping out the human race. Meanwhile, two vengeful superpowered twins seek to turn the Avengers against each other. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Chris Hemsworth, Chris Evans and Scarlett Johansson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble see how koalas get ready to mate and how to outwit the cunning hyena. Jean Johansson takes part in training on the lake to save a man overboard

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An Axminster property that took four years to develop, a small office in Basingstoke crying out to be a house and a Birkenhead terrace that has three - or maybe four - bedrooms

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood joins the US Army's First Armoured Division, travelling into the desert to take part in a field exercise testing the combat readiness of troops and weaponry

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Pulp frontman Jarvis Cocker and the Happy Mondays' Bez take part in this special edition for BBC Music Day. Along with their bandmates Candida Doyle and Rowetta Satchell, they join experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey scouring Kent Showground for items that will make them the most profit for charity. While they're busy, presenter Charlie Ross pays a visit to a recording studio in Wales, Rockfields

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine's self-doubt is addressed by an actor, and a missed deadline puts Kam under pressure

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Katharine Merry, Derek Redmond, Ryan Sidebottom, Paul Robinson and Peter Shilton form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes property shopping in Exmoor with a newlywed couple who have a £625k budget for a home that will house his parents and provide her with land for horses

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Head housekeeper Grace, telemarketing managing director Pierre and vineyard manager Adderley spend a day doing each other's jobs before judging whose is the hardest

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Arusha Irvine and Angus Ashworth tour the West Country, where Roo meets Exmoor's equine inhabitants and Angus bares all at an exclusive beach that can only be accessed by tunnel

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: Suzie Lee's Home Cook Heroes
programme.desc: Suzie cooks a roast chicken dinner in less than an hour using ingredients provided by the Toman family from Belfast. She goes back to her Chinese takeaway roots with sweet and sour chicken, and continues her mission against waste by using the leftovers in a chicken super food salad. To round off, she bakes white chocolate and raspberry choux buns

programme.title: A City Dreaming
programme.desc: Documentary profile of Derry/Londonderry, written and narrated by Gerry Anderson, who referred to his birthplace as 'Stroke City'. The film features a series of personal recollections of the city and its people over half a century of history - a time that saw it rise from poverty and neglect to hitting headlines across the world

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Environment, Food and Rural Affairs Committee's session on flooding with evidence from Emma Howard Boyd, Chair, Environment Agency, Sir James Bevan, Chief Executive, Environment Agency and John Curtin, Executive Director of Flood and Coastal Risk Management, Environment Agency, from Tuesday 14 July

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Conservative MP for South Ribble, gives her maiden speech in the House of Commons, from Monday 29 June.

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Labour MP for Pontypridd, Alex Davies-Jones, gives her maiden speech in the House of Commons, from Wednesday 15 January.

programme.title: Select Committees

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Labour MP for Nottingham East, Nadia Whittome, making her maiden speech in the House of Commons, from Monday 10 January

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Coverage of a statement by Health Minister Nadine Dorries in response to the publication of the Cumberledge Review into medical device and medicine safety. The review looked into how the health system responded to concerns raised by patients about the Primodos hormone pregnancy test, the drug sodium valproate and surgical mesh, from Thursday 9 July

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the Scottish Parliament to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon.

programme.title: Westminster in Review
programme.desc: The Budget, Huawei, Black Lives Matter and Domestic Abuse have occupied much of Parliament's time when it hasn't been dealing with the coronavirus pandemic. Alicia McCarthy looks back at how Westminster has tackled these and other big stories

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Labour MP for Streatham, Bell Ribeiro-Addy, making her maiden speech in the House of Commons, from Thursday 30 January

programme.title: Inside Number 10
programme.desc: In the first in a series of interviews with former cabinet secretaries, political journalist Sue Cameron talks to Lord Armstrong about the role of the most powerful civil servant in the land always at the Prime Minister's shoulder. Filmed in 2018

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: Coverage of debates on issues affecting Scotland

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: SDLP MP for Belfast South, Claire Hanna, making her maiden speech in the House of Commons, from Friday 20 December 2019. First shown on BBC Parliament on Wednesday 19 February

programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to author Ian Beesley about his book 'The Official History of the Cabinet Secretaries'

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The maiden speech in the House of Commons by the new Alliance MP for North Down, Stephen Farry, from Friday 20 December. First shown on BBC Parliament on Saturday 15 February

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Communications Committee session on the future of journalism in the UK, with evidence from the New Statesman, the Spectator, ITV and a former Downing Street communications director, from Tuesday 16 June.

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Liberal Democrats MP for St Albans, Daisy Cooper, making her maiden speech in the House of Commons, from Monday 23 January. First shown on BBC Parliament on Wednesday 19 February

programme.title: Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: A big storm causes a lot of damage on the island of Struay, and Uncle Matthew's home is one of the buildings damaged

programme.title: Bruno
programme.desc: The playful monkey dresses up as a clown and juggles

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Shane takes part in a charity race - but finds training for it hard work

programme.title: Luthaidh (Louie)
programme.desc: The artistic rabbit finds an old treasure map at the seaside

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: One of the animals sets off on an adventure in search of the words 'magnifying glass'

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Alec MacDonald reads Eanraig agus an Yeti, written by Russell Ayto and translated into Gaelic by Donald John MacLeod

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Penfold and Danger Mouse get a taste of Thanksgiving when they join Jeopardy Mouse in America

programme.title: Dràgonan - Rèis chun an iomaill (Dragons: Race to the Edge)
programme.desc: When the Riders uncover the location of Viggo's Dragon Auction, they must stop him from selling dragons and stealing all the gold in Berk

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: Documentary following the activities of Peter, an emperor penguin who lives among 25,000 other penguins on the Ross Ice Shelf in Antarctica

programme.title: O Mo Dhùthaich (From Uist with Love)
programme.desc: Archie and Ben are busy in the lambing shed, Rory goes in search of goose eggs and Morag and Neil are at the cattle sale

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary following student life, including a beer-drinking competition and protests

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Naidheachdan ionadail, nàiseanta is eadar-nàiseanta gach là cuide ri fiosrachadh coimhearsnachd bho BBC ALBA. Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Having made it through a very wet winter, Sweeny now focuses on new life on the croft as he heads into spring. It is one of his favourite times of the year, and it's all go as one of his sows goes into labour, but this is one that he has to keep a close eye on. He also deals with the challenges of lambing; a time of very little sleep and plenty of early starts. With over 50 already lambed he's half way there, but his early morning checks prove vital as he finds out when he needs to give help to one of his sheep which is struggling. This isn't the only sheep he has to help. Sweeny lives on one of the most rugged coastlines in Britain, and although it's beautiful it can be very dangerous for the animals as he knows all to well. He calls the local mountain rescue in to help one of his sheep distressed on the cliff edge

programme.title: Rose Reilly
programme.desc: Profile of the Ayrshire footballer, who became an icon for female players when she led the Italian team to victory in the 1984 women's world cup

programme.title: Eadar Chluich
programme.desc: Short comedy interpretation of Death of the Horse, sung by John 'Hoddan' MacDonald and starring Greg McHugh

programme.title: Annie Oakley
programme.desc: Documentary about the sharpshooter, who taught herself to hunt to support her family and became a celebrity as part of Buffalo Bill's Wild West show

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming in remote and unspoilt lochs and rivers, this time visiting Loch Maree

programme.title: Air an Rathad (On the Road)
programme.desc: Donnie Mackay drives the latest Ford Mustang and meets fans of American classic cars. Linda MacLeod takes the latest Mini Clubman to Torridon to see whether it has come of age

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Live UEFA Women's Champions League
programme.desc: Glasgow City v Wolfsburg (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the one-legged quarter-final from Anoeta Stadium in San Sebastian. Spain

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Naidheachdan ionadail, nàiseanta is eadar-nàiseanta gach là cuide ri fiosrachadh coimhearsnachd bho BBC ALBA. Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Celtic Connections
programme.desc: Dallahan sing Jail Song, a traditional song with additional contemporary lyrics

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: Times are tough for Fraochy Bay's sweet-toothed residents, as the town's excessive sugar consumption leads to the passing of new laws and the introduction of prohibition

programme.title: Piping Live!
programme.desc: Alan MacDonald introduces performances by the Battlefield Band, Ross Miller and Charlie Stewart and Northumberland piper Chris Ormiston

programme.title: Dealbhan Fraoich (Heather's Portraits)
programme.desc: Heather travels to Dunvegan on the Isle of Skye where she meets keen historian Peter MacAskill who has established a number of museums on the island

programme.title: Ceol Duthchasach/Ken Burns' Country Music
programme.desc: Biographies of the trailblazers who created and shaped Country music, from the Carter Family and Bob Wills to Hank Williams, Patsy Cline and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Eadar Chluich
programme.desc: Short comedy interpretation of Death of the Horse, sung by John 'Hoddan' MacDonald and starring Greg McHugh

programme.title: Lorient is Alba: Scotland's Year
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Allan MacDonald look back at the Year of Scotland at the 47th Interceltique Festival of Lorient, in August 2017. Featuring bands and musicians from Scotland and Brittany, including Ho-ro

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: A big storm causes a lot of damage on the island of Struay, and Uncle Matthew's home is one of the buildings damaged

programme.title: Lon le Linda
programme.desc: Patrick and Heidi help Linda to make vegetable curry, and learn about how cauliflowers are grown

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: Mossy wants the gang to join him on a food-hunt but they are all called on a different sort of search when Pip and Pop decide to hide on poor Otto

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Micawber is in need of a bath, but first Pàdraig must get the bath to the lighthouse

programme.title: Dràgonan - Rèis chun an iomaill (Dragons: Race to the Edge)
programme.desc: When the Riders uncover the location of Viggo's Dragon Auction, they must stop him from selling dragons and stealing all the gold in Berk

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Everyone should be careful today as Fearchar is attempting archery

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Anna reveals her very unusual hobby

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports

programme.title: 'San Fhuil (It's in the Blood)
programme.desc: Made from rock as old as the moon, Uist has been shaped by winds, tides and the people who have continuously worked this landscape for 5,000 years. The traditional crofting methods enable rare species of birds and flowers to thrive that are extinct elsewhere in the British Isles.

programme.title: Druthag na Hearadh (A Little Drop of Harris)
programme.desc: A few technical glitches get sorted out as the team draws on local resources and knowledge before the distillery opens for business. The first festive season sees the community centred on the distillery peat fire, and history is made when the first Harris whisky spirit is distilled

programme.title: Bonn Còmhraidh
programme.desc: Donald Morrison examines the impact the sea had on the Gaelic community during the 1970s, which was a period of change for the River Clyde. Plus, Martin MacDonald reports the herring port at Mallaig, which was one of the last of its kind on the west coast

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: The soldiers of the special forces are still trying to find their way home and a survival expert is keen to share his skills

programme.title: Puirt-adhair (Highland Airports)
programme.desc: Things are busy in Stornoway for the fire fighters, the Loganair team and Dooley in the cafe. The Highland Gliding Club is hosting the first Scottish competition of the season, and Andrew Bruce is kept on the hop fuelling a range of aircraft in Wick

programme.title: Ceol Duthchasach/Ken Burns' Country Music
programme.desc: Biographies of the trailblazers who created and shaped Country music, from the Carter Family and Bob Wills to Hank Williams, Patsy Cline and Garth Brooks

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming in remote and unspoilt lochs and rivers, this time visiting Loch Maree

programme.title: Ever Believe Me - Sgeulachd Madeleine Smith (The Madeleine Smith Story)
programme.desc: Documentary revisiting the 19th-century murder case involving Madeleine Smith, asking whether the Glasgow socialite really did poison her lover with arsenic. Presented by John Morrison

programme.title: Annie Oakley
programme.desc: Documentary about the sharpshooter, who taught herself to hunt to support her family and became a celebrity as part of Buffalo Bill's Wild West show

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Music from acclaimed band Skerryvore, performing Take My Hand at the Trad Awards

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice. Presented by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani presents coverage of day two of the Ebor Festival, featuring the 3.15 Yorkshire Oaks and support races at 1.45, 2.15, 2.45 and 3.45. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports by Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Mick FitzGerald, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Simon, Matt, Julie and Kelly pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale Family Tree
programme.desc: A look back at three of the soap's most-loved families, weaving its way through each family tree to reveal the loves and losses that the parents, sibiings and children have faced over five decades of drama. Up first are the Dingles, who first hit the screens in the mid-1990s. Led by bare-knuckle fighter Zak, they were not exactly nice people, but eventually they became popular with viewers and became the scheming family they are today. Gaynor Fay narrates this look back at the life of Zak, his three wives and his seven children

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: After the unprecedented double hit of coronavirus and lockdown, can local businesses and high streets survive? From barbers to bookshops, restaurants to fashion retailers - reporter Adam Shaw investigates are planning their bounce back - and hears from people keen to get back to work in an uncertain job market

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher's walk around the Wales Coast Path reaches Carmarthen Bay and the Gower Peninsula, an area known for its peaceful estuaries, sandy beaches and limestone cliffs. At Pendine Sands he hears the story of Wales' land-speed record holder and sees his original car roar up the beach once more. Sean also tries to beat the tide and reach the end of Worm's Head, the westerly tip of Gower

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the doors of British factories to find out how they keep shelves stocked, from massive factories making meals in their millions to family firms trading on secret recipes. Tonight's edition celebrates a few British classics, including a family firm that has been churning out Stilton cheese for three generations. Cameras also go behind the scenes of Lea & Perrins to see the making of their world-famous Worcestershire Sauce, as well as coffee-roasting at Taylors of Harrogate

programme.title: Inside Asprey: Luxury by Royal Appointment
programme.desc: The daily workings of the high-end London designer, which has supplied jewellery to the royal family since the reign of Queen Victoria. Over one summer on New Bond Street, the sales team tempts VIP customers with one-of-a-kind, gem-encrusted handbags selling for an average price of £33,000, while in the workshop above, silversmiths are seen making much of Asprey's luxury merchandise by hand. The programme also highlights the challenge of crafting a three-piece jewellery suite to hit the shop floor on schedule

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including the heart-stopping story of a man who took a ‘pleasure flight' on a hang-glider. Two passengers on board the Viking Sky cruise ship that suffered engine failure in rough seas just off the coast of Norway recount how they were left fearing for their lives, while a heroic cop throws the rulebook out of the window when trying to save a baby's life on the roadside

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five university friends answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: After the unprecedented double hit of coronavirus and lockdown, can local businesses and high streets survive? From barbers to bookshops, restaurants to fashion retailers - reporter Adam Shaw investigates are planning their bounce back - and hears from people keen to get back to work in an uncertain job market

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie offers Nate his job back, plus a bonus if he agrees to keep the secret. Dan is panicking as he tries to keep his financial troubles a secret from Amelia, and Leyla offers Gabby an apprenticeship at Take a Vow

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Seb is shocked to discover Abi is in hospital, and Mary, Sean and Billy stake out Victoria Garden, waiting for Todd to approach the money bag. While Gemma tries to keep Paul's party a secret, Daniel's obsession with Nicky continues, much to her discomfort

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A fisherman's body is found tangled up in a North Sea trawler's net. Vera and her team investigate with the help of the local harbourmaster, and find the dead man's sons pointing the finger at the owner of a fish market, with whom they have a long-running feud. Retracing the dead man's steps, Vera discovers when he drowned and tracks down a waitress who may have been one of the last people to see him alive. However, the case becomes more complicated when a witness runs away and a boat belonging to the dead man's sister-in-law goes up in flames

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Virgin Media's V Festival 2020
programme.desc: As the UK faces a summer of cancelled festivals and gigs, Virgin Media's V Festival returns with a virtual twist. Joel Dommett and Maya Jama present festival fun from Hylands Park in Chelmsford, with exclusive performances from Olly Murs, Anne-Marie and Dizzee Rascal. Featuring exclusive interviews, festival fashion, festival food and a look back at some of the event's most memorable performances

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the second episode, Stephen is joined by guests Joel Dommett, Chris Kamara, and Gemma Collins

programme.title: 1000 Heartbeats
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is auditioning for a circus troop, but he is actually in a professional wrestling ring, in the middle of a fight!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he is in the subway but actually, he's in a cave. Running into Weasel, he thinks he's lost and decides to help him find his way.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has reduced the size of the moon in order to model it in his own image. But Magoo thinks it's a dinosaur egg ready to hatch!

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: An accident at an asteroid-based mining facility sends the rock hurtling toward the Sun, with crewman Ned Tedford still on-site. Alan and Kayo deploy Thunderbird Three for the rescue mission, but when a rogue solar flare knocks out their sensors, they are forced to find a way to alter the asteroid's trajectory so that it loops around the sun instead of plummeting to its ultra-hot surface. Animated adventure, with the voices of Rasmus Hardiker and Angel Coulby

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is thinks is out trick-or-treating for Halloween. But he unwittingly robs a bank instead of Weasel.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is a part-time firefighter reservist whose rescue mission for the day is to save a cat in a tree. In reality, the cat is none other than Fizz.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Thinking he is in the middle of a work-out at his local gym, Magoo inadvertently climbs onto a delivery boy's bicycle.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike decides to let his artistic side out and sculpt a statue of Iris, but things don't go to plan.

programme.title: Buck & Buddy
programme.desc: Beetle Buck and stick insect Buddy are the best of friends who like to collect unusual and interesting things from the strange world in which they live.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Inspired by a documentary on Buckingham Palace, Bean sets out to decorate his room in royal style, and landlady Mrs Wicket suffers delusions of grandeur

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Guilt-ridden over having lost the Traveler's Tea, Cole embarks on a dangerous quest to the top of a nearby mountain in hopes of finding the legendary Traveler's Tree.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: All Ben wants to do is stop Queen Bee from robbing a bank.

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Bunni concludes that Maggie is a witch and organises a bogus event to expose her, but the tables are turned on Bunni soon she is branded as a witch.

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Bunni thinks she's got the Champs cornered when Europe's Most Haunted Hotel TV show comes to film in the B and B. Can the Champs survive a team of crack TV ghost hunters?

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by guests Roman Kemp and Vick Hope, and chefs Marcus Wareing, Jose Pizarro and Bettina Campolucci Bordi. Featuring recipes for chicken pithivier and cheesecake, and a masterclass in the best way to prepare steak

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James samples the culinary delights of Northern Ireland with friend and fellow chef Paul Rankin, visiting a wasabi farm and a honey producer. James cooks overlooking the dramatic backdrop of Slieve Gullion - the highest point in Armagh - before the pair visit Lough Neagh

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Ben Fogle tries his hand at competitive sheep shearing in north Wales and Liz Bonnin is on the Isle of Mull, spotting pods of migrating dolphins who are returning to the shores. Plus, a visit to the Sussex home of Zoe Ball, who gives a tour of her own back garden before heading off to the National Trust's Standen House for some tips on how to make her garden grow

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the fourth and final day of the 2020 Ebor Festival, including the feature race, the 3.35 Skybet Ebor. Other races at York include the 1.50 Strensall Stakes, 2.25 Melrose Handicap and 3.00 City of York Stakes. There's also action from Sandown Park, including some very competitive handicap races. Analysis by Jason Weaver, reports from Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Mick Fitzgerald, and commentary by Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shepherd

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: The five remaining acts compete for a place in the final while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the third episode, Stephen is joined by guests John Thomson, Vick Hope and Joe Swash

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the semi-final of the junior talent contest. At the end of the Battle Rounds, each of the four judges has selected three acts to stay in the contest. These 12 solo singers and double acts must now give it their all for the chance to be their mentor's choice for next week's grand final, and get the chance to win a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort. Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am make up the regular judging panel

programme.title: Licence to Kill (1989)
programme.desc: While on leave to attend the wedding of his friend, CIA agent Felix Leiter, James Bond is asked to help with a secret mission to capture notorious drug baron, Franz Sanchez. Though successful, Sanchez later escapes and seeks revenge on Leiter, killing his new bride and leaving Leiter for dead. Incensed by this, Bond refuses his next mission from MI6 to go in search of Sanchez, leaving M with no alternative but to revoke Bond's licence to kill. Now considered a rogue agent, Bond goes undercover to pose as an assassin for hire within Sanchez's drug empire, to bring him down from the inside. Spy thriller, starring Timothy Dalton, Robert Davi, Carey Lowell, Talisa Soto and Benicio Del Toro

programme.title: Virgin Media's V Festival 2020
programme.desc: Joel Dommett and Maya Jama present festival fun from Hylands Park in Chelmsford, with exclusive performances from Olly Murs, Anne-Marie and Dizzee Rascal. Each episode sees the artists surprise their fans watching the festival from their living rooms. Plus, a look back at some of the most memorable performances from V Festival

programme.title: Small Fortune
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts a celebrity version of the game show, with a team comprising Richard Madeley, his daughter Chloe, and her husband rugby star James Haskell taking on challenges in miniature settings to win £150,000 for charity

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A fisherman's body is found tangled up in a North Sea trawler's net. Vera and her team investigate with the help of the local harbourmaster, and find the dead man's sons pointing the finger at the owner of a fish market, with whom they have a long-running feud. Retracing the dead man's steps, Vera discovers when he drowned and tracks down a waitress who may have been one of the last people to see him alive. However, the case becomes more complicated when a witness runs away and a boat belonging to the dead man's sister-in-law goes up in flames

programme.title: Licence to Kill (1989)
programme.desc: While on leave to attend the wedding of his friend, CIA agent Felix Leiter, James Bond is asked to help with a secret mission to capture notorious drug baron, Franz Sanchez. Though successful, Sanchez later escapes and seeks revenge on Leiter, killing his new bride and leaving Leiter for dead. Incensed by this, Bond refuses his next mission from MI6 to go in search of Sanchez, leaving M with no alternative but to revoke Bond's licence to kill. Now considered a rogue agent, Bond goes undercover to pose as an assassin for hire within Sanchez's drug empire, to bring him down from the inside. Spy thriller, starring Timothy Dalton, Robert Davi, Carey Lowell, Talisa Soto and Benicio Del Toro

The pair must work out if this is a sign of rivalry taken too far, or is the killer driven by something darker?

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The discovery of slaves on the Pritchard farm has turned a murder inquiry into a multi-agency operation. The enormity of the case brings Dodson to Syndicate 9, keen to establish Gill's state of mind and efficacy as senior officer. Having been the driver involved in the fatal collision with Cal Pritchard, Janet is under investigation and unable to interview the man's widow Evie. Devastated by the consequences of her actions, the detective eventually finds solace in Chris's calm and kind reasoning, but when an incandescent Evie smells alcohol on Gill's breath, she has all the ammunition she needs to discredit the case and bring the DCI down

programme.desc: Jean's sister visits, but she is not included in Sid's pre-arranged plans - and he is not about to alter them. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Crane and Merton have their hands full when a sniper strikes during a clay-pigeon shoot, and Bellamy's fiery temper only serves to make matters worse. Elsewhere, Vernon decides to venture into the B&B market - but his cooking skills leave a lot to be desired. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of Kim's funeral arrives. The Dingles kidnap one of the thugs and hold him hostage. Biff and Linda are shocked to find an abandoned baby on their doorstep

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff and Linda take the abandoned baby to the police. DI Cooke questions Zoe about the cufflink. Sophie clashes with Chris after he gets drunk

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally asks Mike for a job as a trainee machinist. The Council agrees to rename Coronation Street after Alf. Steve is pleased to hear Fiona and Tony have split

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Crane and Merton have their hands full when a sniper strikes during a clay-pigeon shoot, and Bellamy's fiery temper only serves to make matters worse. Elsewhere, Vernon decides to venture into the B&B market - but his cooking skills leave a lot to be desired. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Julian Fellowes bids farewell to the Crawleys with one last festive special, catching up with the Abbey's residents at the tail-end of 1925. Mary endeavours to build bridges, but Edith's secret continues to pose a threat. As Henry settles into the role of husband, scars left by Charlie's death are slow to heal, while Carson struggles to come to terms with developments that prove even he is not invulnerable to change. Spratt's moonlighting venture is threatened by the conniving of Denker, and Daisy may have missed her chance with an admirer. Isobel receives devastating news, while the day that Anna and Bates have long-since waited for arrives

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The GP is left holding the baby after upsetting the new childminder, but luckily Mike agrees to look after James at short notice. However, Louisa is not happy with the arrangement and an argument erupts between the newlyweds just as their guests, Dennis and Karen Dodds, arrive for dinner - and that's only the first of the evening's problems. Meanwhile, Ruth is roped into taking part in a phone-in programme on Radio Portwenn and unwittingly answers a call from PC Penhale, who wants to know how to make friends in the village

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: A secret admirer makes Ruth feel a little uncomfortable when he places a red rose on her car windscreen and then leaves a birthday cake on her kitchen table while she's at the radio station, and when matters take a turn for the worse, Martin has no choice but to take drastic action. Meanwhile, Bert is furious when one of Louisa's pupils writes a damning review of his restaurant in the school newspaper, and a new pharmacist arrives in the village. Annabelle Apsion (Shameless) joins the cast

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The start of a shooting rampage through the streets of London leaves a nightclub peppered with bullets and two revellers dead. With the media swarming all over the case and a hostage to track down, Brooks and Devlin race against the clock to find the duo responsible for the massacre, but their defence barrister (Lesley Manville) claims one of them was more innocent victim than violent killer. With Brooke Kinsella

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Lewis joins Morse on a trip to Australia, but it is no holiday - they are pursuing a former supergrass who holds the key to a mystery back in Oxford. Going deep into the heart of the Outback, Morse soon begins to miss the comforts of home, particularly his regular tipple, although Lewis finds he has more in common with his Aussie brethren than he thought. Crime drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Con O'Neill

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid wants a lie-in, but it is doomed from the beginning as Sally chases romance and Jean goes on a diet. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid resolves to exercise a little discipline when Sally adopts a stray dog. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Comedy series. When a storm makes him homeless again, Wilfred has to find a new dwelling

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid suffers temporary amnesia, but Jean is on hand to produce reminders of his antics at the annual Boy Scouts reunion. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Newlyweds Mike and Ellie decide to build their dream home at a beauty spot called Gypsy's Acre, ignoring the warnings of elusive romany Mrs Lee about an ancient curse supposedly cast upon the land. The couple settle into their new life, but the mysterious woman makes her presence felt, subjecting them to a campaign of hatred in an attempt to force them out. Tragedy soon strikes, but as luck would have it Miss Marple is staying nearby with her recently widowed old friend Mrs Phillpot. However, if the spinster sleuth is to solve the puzzle, she must put her own life in grave danger. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with Wendy Craig, Glynis Barber, Hugh Dennis, Tamzin Outhwaite, Tom Hughes, Joanna Vanderham, Aneurin Barnard, William Hope and Janet Henfrey

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: On the day of Gary's wedding, David tells him he needs to make a decision about whether he loves Sarah or Maria. Tim informs Geoff that he and Sally are going away to stay with Gina, and Gemma announces that she's going to set up an online mother and baby group

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi has an accident, and an oblivious Seb thinks his mum has let him down as the twins prepare to leave for Australia. Meanwhile, Paul is disappointed with Billy over his 30th birthday plans, and Mary and Sean are shocked to discover a letter from Todd to Eileen demanding money

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Seb is shocked to discover Abi is in hospital, and Mary, Sean and Billy stake out Victoria Garden, waiting for Todd to approach the money bag. While Gemma tries to keep Paul's party a secret, Daniel's obsession with Nicky continues, much to her discomfort

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker (My Family)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An American female bishop visiting St Gerard's College is found dead after drinking poisoned wine, and when another two killings occur, both mirroring macabre murders from Jacobean revenge tragedy, it appears the culprit is targeting candidates in the election to become vice-regent. However, when Innocent learns one of the suspects is hiding a dark secret, Lewis and Hathaway realise the motive is much more twisted. Guest starring Sian Phillips and Ronald Pickup, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover a dead body surrounded by rabbits that have escaped from a local pet show, after someone deliberately freed them from their cages. At the crime scene, DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover that the victim died from a stab wound to the neck, and theorise that the murderer might be a competitor sabotaging the Belville Small Pet Show, and question the Best in Show defending champion Timothy Benson. The pair must work out if this is a sign of rivalry taken too far, or is the killer driven by something darker?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The life of an eccentric and reclusive couple comes under police scrutiny when a social services investigator is killed, and Barnaby must unearth generations of family secrets and decipher astronomical charts to find the murderer. Meanwhile, the detective's wife Sarah (Fiona Dolman, The Royal Today) arrives in Midsomer as the new head of a local school. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with Phyllida Law, Edward Fox and Neil Pearson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When the actress's more famous sister Diana returns after a 40-year family rift, the body count rises, with each crime reminiscent of a movie plot - giving Barnaby and Jones what looks like a disturbing case of life imitating art. Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes star, with Harriet Walter and Sinead Cusack

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A body is found with a note reading 'They Must Be Destroyed' - a chilling reminder of a case Brooks was involved in six years previously, when extreme racist Andrew Dillon killed three boys from ethnic minorities. Steel is then put under intense scrutiny when it is revealed that his success in convicting Dillon was largely due to selective prosecution. Bradley Walsh and Ben Daniels star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate a hospital department where three patients have died in suspicious circumstances in six months - the latest being a woman brought into A&E with flu-like symptoms. The detectives learn she died after being given a lethal cocktail of prescription drugs - but with an unsigned patient chart and an extremely busy evening on the ward, tracking down the killer proves challenging. Crime drama, guest starring James Fox, with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a toddler goes missing from a high-street merry-go-round, his distraught mother reports him kidnapped and a massive police hunt is launched to track him down. However, with more than one suspect and no physical evidence, the search throws up more questions than answers - especially as the boy's mum seems to be hiding something. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The Regent's Canal runs through the heart of London linking the docks with the rest of Britain. John navigates the history-filled route, finding the East End's haunted Ragged School, and making and sampling a bottle or two of 'mother's ruin' along the way. The veteran broadcaster even finds time to stop off to feed the giraffes at London Zoo.

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike attempts to earn some cash without Sid's interference. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James
