programme.desc: Uncle Marvin reveals he has become a stockbroker and convinces Barry and his friends to invest their money with him. Beverly invites 50 people over for a Thanksgiving 'potluck' dinner instead of cooking for the family

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Murray insists that a reluctant Adam must have driving lessons, the family learns something new about Pops in the process. Erica's grumpy nature means she has a hard time making friends at college and she turns down an invitation to a toga party, but when Barry visits her for the weekend, he quickly thrives on the college social scene

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam and Barry decide to write a song like the iconic Parents Just Don't Understand. But when they don't get the reaction they expected from Murray, the boys take steps to make amends. Beverly keeps calling Erica at college, but Erica wants some space from her mother and screens her phone calls, forcing Beverly to adopt drastic measure

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Erica tells Beverly she is going to spend the first night of Hanukkah with Geoff and his family, Beverly starts a competition over who hosts the best Hanukkah party. Meanwhile, Barry seeks advice from his grandfather about how to be cool and Pops suggests that he should enter the school's holiday talent show

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny hosts a Halloween costume party, which proves a worthy test for the boys' social skills. During the event, Leonard has a run-in with Penny's ex-boyfriend. Kaley Cuoco, Johnny Galecki and Simon Helberg star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj's parents set him up on a blind date in the hope of initiating an arranged marriage, but after a promising start it becomes apparent that the girl in question is more interested in Sheldon. Meanwhile, Penny begins her new job as a bartender. Starring Kunal Nayyar and Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard, Raj and Howard kick Sheldon off their physics quiz team and replace him with Leslie Winkle, so he decides to prove his superiority by forming a rival quartet of his own. Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny starts her own hair accessories business and the orders start to pile in. However, she makes the mistake of asking Sheldon to help, leading to frustration and regret. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny catches the eye of comic-book shopkeeper Stuart, and Leonard is devastated when she gives serious consideration to dating her new admirer. Desperate for help, Leonard enlists Sheldon to discredit his love rival by preying on his obsession with Batman. Johnny Galecki, Kaley Cuoco and Kevin Sussman star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard tells Penny he loves her, but fails to get the response he would have liked. Sheldon also finds himself competing against Star Trek: The Next Generation actor Wil Wheaton once again when the gang goes bowling - and tries to get his revenge

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The guys have fun at the expense of Penny's boyfriend Zack, but later try to make amends by enlisting his help with the New Year's costume competition at the comic-book store. Comedy, starring Kaley Cuoco and Simon Helberg

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard returns from his research expedition in the North Sea and undergoes an adjustment period with Sheldon. Howard experiences an unusual emotional reaction as a result of his relationship with his mother. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Simon Helberg star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Tensions rise when the guys struggle to complete their Air Force project on time and are forced to work around the clock - leaving Sheldon fighting to stay awake. Penny and Amy attend a university gathering at the home of Bert the geologist. Raj learns Bernadette's true feelings about her pregnancy when he helps her to clear out the spare room for a nursery

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Cut off from his father's fortune, Raj struggles to balance his books, so decides to appoint Sheldon to organise his finances and get him out of debt. Penny considers joining the guys at Comic-Con because she thinks Leonard wants her to come along

programme.title: Transformers: Age of Extinction (2014)
programme.desc: The shape-changing robots are driven into hiding when the human race turns against them. When rediscovered by a kindly inventor and his daughter, they come out of exile to save the world, battling a new breed of human-created Transformers. These deadly new machines were created to hunt them down, but are now growing independent and intend to make use of technology from the Transformers' home planet in a plot that could threaten all life on Earth. Fourth film in the sci-fi adventure series, starring Mark Wahlberg, Stanley Tucci and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Central Intelligence (2016)
programme.desc: Action comedy, starring Dwayne Johnson as a one-time geek who suffered constant humiliation at high school but grew up to be a lethal CIA agent. But when he is targeted by his own organisation, he comes home for a high school reunion, where he enlists the help of the former campus tough guy - now a meek accountant - who very quickly wonders what he has got himself into. Co-starring Kevin Hart and Amy Ryan

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Shows including Ninja Warrior UK, Britain's Got Talent, Hostile Planet, This Morning, Game of Thrones, Years and Years, Bear's Mission with David Walliams and Bake Off: The Professionals come under scrutiny by the armchair critics, as they share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week. Narrated by Craig Cash

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of shows from May 2019, including EastEnders, Countdown, The Eurovision Song Contest, Gentleman Jack and the Game of Thrones finale. Narrated by Craig Cash

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: American-born 32-year-old magazine editor Alex arrives sporting blazing blue hair, but is nervous about how her date will react when she reveals the reasons behind her unique new look. Her date, 33-year-old professional storyteller Will, can speak to thousands of people at once without worry - but do the nerves hit when he's one on one with his date? Teacher Masackia, who's 29, doesn't want exotic food on her plate but she would like an exotic man. Her date with 41-year-old Dieter might be a lesson in love as they learn about each other's nuances and share stories from their pasts, but as differences begin to appear, will their date pass its final test?

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A model with dreams of becoming a singer seeks a man who can help her feel more confident in herself, and may find what she is looking for in an aspiring fashion designer. An 80-year-old man tries to convince his date that the mistakes that led to his three divorces are in the past, and a 26-year-old woman who already has her wedding planned looks for an affluent older man to complete the picture

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: In the middle of a nationwide shortage of liquid helium, Sheldon and Leonard take desperate measures to obtain the supplies they need in order to prove a scientific theory before a rival team of researchers can beat them to it. Meanwhile, Penny and Bernadette download a dating app onto Amy's phone in order to find her a new boyfriend

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: While being interviewed for a documentary about Star Trek character Mr Spock, Sheldon struggles to suppress his emotions about his recent break-up with Amy, leaving Leonard and Penny shocked to discover he had bought her an engagement ring. Howard and Bernadette fall out over plans to renovate his childhood home. Wil Wheaton, Adam Nimoy and Stephen Merchant guest star

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Joe puts all his thought into a birthday gift for Mel - so when she ends up not liking it, she does her best to please him by pretending she is happy with it. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel organises a family game night, pitting herself and Joe against Lennox and Zander, but the competition leads to a trip down memory lane when they reminisce about the past. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel launches a healthy diet campaign while running for a Senate seat, but a voter soon catches her out eating some unhealthy fast food. When the man tweets a photo of her gorging on a burger and calls her a hypocrite, she gets into a war of words with him

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel receives unexpected news that could have a direct impact on her attempt to run for a senate seat. Ryder receives his orders from the US Navy, and a car crash gives Lenin and Zander a new outlook on life. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp competes in the Something New from Something Old competition at the Royal Wales Show, creating vintage paper jewellery, but finds herself up against stiff competition

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Celia Thompson has long dreamed of running an arts and crafts business in the Carcassonne area of France. However, her partner Nick Hares is hesitant about leaving the UK, as his teenage daughters live near Cambridge. He'd rather move to the Waveney Valley, on the border of Suffolk and Norfolk. Jonnie Irwin and Laura Hamilton help the couple solve their dilemma

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel to East Sussex in their hunt to discover valuable items 'lost' in people's homes, with their finds including some Victorian street lamps

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Experts Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain to find, fix and flog unwanted possessions on behalf of their owners, turning items into hard cash. A butter churn, a slot machine and a set of print trays are among the finds in this edition

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Couple Mariam and Johnno arrive at their new chateau and discover they have no running water, and Julia has a decision to make when she re-visits a chateaux she fell in love with

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Tom and Georgina in Berkshire, who are planning to renovate a run-down bungalow

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: A quartet of B&B owners battle it out to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money, beginning with luxury weight-loss and detox retreat Slimmeria in East Sussex. The guests worry about uncomfortable sleeping arrangements, the price of accommodation and the strict 9pm curfew - and the prospect of a dance-style workout only exacerbates their bad mood

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jan and William Cheeseman, owners of five-star Gold-rated 38 St Giles in Norwich, try to impress their competitors. Unfortunately, poor breakfast presentation and complaints about noise from the street increase the tension between the couple as they strive for perfection

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ann and Bill Clay play host at their four-star Bosvean House B&B in Bude, Cornwall. Diane and Max Seivewright query the establishment's luxury description, while Galia Grainger complains about the lack of a sea view. However, the hosts hope an indulgent Cornish cream tea, a stick dance and a drink in their garden shed will succeed in winning over the guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Village bakers Diane and Max Seivewright, the owners of Ombersley B&B in Worcestershire, host the last round of the competition, but some guests are far from impressed with the rooms and food

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners gather to find out who has won the competition. Diane and Max Seivewright, the hosts at Ombersley B&B, feel betrayed by Ann and Bill Clay's feedback and want to know why they have been so harshly criticised, while Galia Grainger asks why Slimmeria's rules and regulations have provoked so much hostility

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Spider monkey Mary is 50 and has had 19 children, and the keepers worry that another pregnancy could be risky, so they introduce younger female Kiara to the group in the hope that Mary's long-term partner Popoyan will develop a relationship with her instead. The staff also hope to breed dominant female giant otter Icana with new arrival Diego and plan to bring two feuding sloths closer together, while setting up a speed-dating event for three-legged chameleon Tripod

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team fight to save the leg of a much-loved golden retriever, and a French bulldog with twisted elbows needs a doggy wheelchair and some challenging surgery. At Noel's Oncology & Soft Tissue Hospital in Guildford, a spaniel cross has a tumour removed by specialist Professor Nick Bacon

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul desperately need to fill their lake with fresh water ready for the artists' retreat they are hosting. Emma steps in to model Victorian outfits for the artists, while Paul struggles on kitchen duty. Angel gives Abbie and Karen tips on how to save money for their wedding business, and she shows them how to create a stunning bar

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Architect Patrick Bradley has come up with an unusual £100,000 house design built out of four 45ft shipping containers, welded together to form a giant cross and cantilevered over the top of a stream on the family farm in Co Derry. His mother is hoping the new home will help her son find a girlfriend, but the small budget and tight schedule soon pose problems. Kevin McCloud follows his progress

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a Yorkshire couple who plan to transform a goods wagon into a summerhouse and gin bar, and visits a narrowboat conversion in Buckinghamshire. George's Spanish tour reaches Madrid, where he meets a man who has spent the past 50 years building a cathedral by hand. Plus, work continues on the expandable caravan pod

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Tree surgeon Kevin, 57, arrives at King's College Hospital in London with breathing difficulties, having fallen off a ladder onto a metal bar, while 11-year-old Archie has cut his lip in an accident at school and is worried about stitches. Elsewhere, a drink-related fall has left 53-year-old John with a head injury, and barman Ross, 31, needs treatment for a fractured eye socket following an alcohol-fuelled night out

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, with David Mitchell and Cariad Lloyd taking on Jon Richardson and Russell Howard. Sam Simmons joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The Craggy Island clerics face the prospect of an untimely death during the return flight from a visit to a holy shrine. Jack quickly commandeers the only parachute and bails out, taking the drinks trolley with him - leaving Ted and Dougal to their fate

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The cleric tries to nurture Chris, the hot favourite to win the King of the Sheep festival, back to top form after he is left shaken by a terrifying encounter. Meanwhile, Dougal becomes convinced Craggy Island is being terrorised by a monster

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Tom and Georgina in Berkshire, who are planning to renovate a run-down bungalow

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Tree surgeon Kevin, 57, arrives at King's College Hospital in London with breathing difficulties, having fallen off a ladder onto a metal bar, while 11-year-old Archie has cut his lip in an accident at school and is worried about stitches. Elsewhere, a drink-related fall has left 53-year-old John with a head injury, and barman Ross, 31, needs treatment for a fractured eye socket following an alcohol-fuelled night out

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The Craggy Island clerics face the prospect of an untimely death during the return flight from a visit to a holy shrine. Jack quickly commandeers the only parachute and bails out, taking the drinks trolley with him - leaving Ted and Dougal to their fate

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp enters the single-cake class at the Devon County Show, and hopes to win over the judges with her own Caribbean-inspired recipe. However, it is a fellow competitor's beetroot cake that ends up raising their eyebrows

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Karin Lorenz would love to start a new life in south-western France. She wants to find the right house, with scope to run a self-catering business, so she can get it underway before moving there. However, her partner Mike Griffin would prefer to live in Pembrokeshire. Whichever destination they plump for, Karin's mother is set to move with them - as will their two dogs. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin show the pair some tempting properties

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien repurposes some old suitcases, while Henry Cole gives an old dresser some serious bling

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien discover items including stained glass and a Victorian hay rack that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul desperately need to fill their lake with fresh water ready for the artists' retreat they are hosting. Emma steps in to model Victorian outfits for the artists, while Paul struggles on kitchen duty. Angel gives Abbie and Karen tips on how to save money for their wedding business, and she shows them how to create a stunning bar

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Phil and Adrienne, who are planning a modern home in West Yorkshire. The project is made difficult by the beautiful but tricky sloping plot, but they are not easily dissuaded. However, as they set about building, a family tragedy looks set to stop them in their tracks

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former pop singer Danny Karne welcomes guests to the Pavilion, his fashion and rock 'n' roll-themed hotel in central London, and the sports car parked outside wows his visitors. But the petrol-head's decision to take his fellow hoteliers to a motoring museum goes down badly when one of them reveals he has no passion for cars

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: John Luce and his partner Pat Sutton invite their fellow hoteliers to the Fox and Duck, a country inn in Therfield, Hertfordshire. The local golf club provides the guests with adequate entertainment, and they appear to bond as they share friendly banter and a spot of singing at the end of the night. However, the following morning brings with it a shower disaster

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave and Debbie Lang, who run the New Inn in Newbridge-on-Wye, Powys, play host. Dave is a keen pig breeder and owns the on-site butcher's shop as well as running a pub, so he serves some of his award-winning pork products for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Kerstin and Robert Sharpe, the owners of Westleigh Farm in Somerset, play host, and despite the initial cobwebs found by some of the guests, they are soon won over by the size of the showers, the quality of the milk and the generous helpings for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Golden poison frog Pablo has lost interest in his partner Maria, so 'bad boy' El Loco is brought in to shake things up. In the chimpanzee habitat, there is a power struggle for the role of dominant female, and baby porcupine Marion fends off the meerkat mob

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team face an emergency when a black Labrador is rushed in after being hit by a car. The pioneering vets also treat a cocker spaniel with a bulging disc in its spine and a basset hound that needs its leg straightening. However, the dog is also overweight, so the professor insists it be put on a diet

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul plan to host locals - including the mayor - to view the work they have done on the front of their chateau. However, Paul has first to design, build and, with a bit of advice from Dick Strawbridge, fit two heavy balconies from scratch. Elsewhere, Mariam and Johnno get their antique fireplace fitted, create a stylish sink out of a copper bucket, and host a 'thank you' meal for their builders

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Tracy and Steve Fox want to build an 'urban shed' featuring two studios, three bedrooms and a double-height space with a walkway, made out of industrial-style materials including corrugated cement fibreboard and 20ft panels of polycarbonate sheeting. Having discovered an old milk yard tucked away behind some flats and shops in south-east London, their long-held desire to build a highly unconventional family home and workspace begins. But their choice of unorthodox materials proves challenging and costly. Kevin McCloud follows their progress

programme.title: Code 37: Sex Crimes
programme.desc: Hannah's personal quest takes on a new dimension after Robert's death - she's convinced that he was murdered and won't rest until she gets the perpetrator. Meanwhile, the body of ex-escort Loulou is discovered on a construction site. With Hannah absent, Greet works on the case with Charles, Bob and Kevin. Belgian crime thriller, starring Veerle Baetens, Michael Pas, Marc Lauwrys and Gilles De Schryver. In Flemish

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Staff nurse Graeme is attacked by a prisoner who has been brought to King's College Hospital for treatment and the team has to restrain the patient before giving him the care he needs. Father-of-two Tony, 59, is rushed in by helicopter medics following a head-on traffic collision, 85-year-old Eric arrives with ongoing stomach problems and 79-year-old Jim, who is experiencing breathing difficulties, quickly strikes up a rapport with the female staff and muses on what it means to be a gentleman

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A young man who has had a severe reaction to a recreational drug is wheeled into resus by paramedics, and senior sister Jen and the medical team face a challenge to calm their patient down so they can treat him. A 65-year-old woman is brought in by her best friends after hitting her head when she fell off a chair while playing bingo, and a 31-year-old man with multiple sclerosis is rushed in after developing a fever at home

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Philadelphia to help failing Mexican restaurant Zocalo. Owners Greg and Mary Russell met and fell in love while they were working there, and the place held such sentimental importance to them they pooled their money and bought it. Now Gordon tries to turn things around before the floundering business ruins their marriage and tears their family apart

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Phil and Adrienne, who are planning a modern home in West Yorkshire. The project is made difficult by the beautiful but tricky sloping plot, but they are not easily dissuaded. However, as they set about building, a family tragedy looks set to stop them in their tracks

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Staff nurse Graeme is attacked by a prisoner who has been brought to King's College Hospital for treatment and the team has to restrain the patient before giving him the care he needs. Father-of-two Tony, 59, is rushed in by helicopter medics following a head-on traffic collision, 85-year-old Eric arrives with ongoing stomach problems and 79-year-old Jim, who is experiencing breathing difficulties, quickly strikes up a rapport with the female staff and muses on what it means to be a gentleman

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The cleric tries to nurture Chris, the hot favourite to win the King of the Sheep festival, back to top form after he is left shaken by a terrifying encounter. Meanwhile, Dougal becomes convinced Craggy Island is being terrorised by a monster

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: In this bite-sized episode, Ellie and Nick Harrington are desperate to revamp their neglected kitchen and dining room in their Tunbridge Wells home. Kirstie Allsopp heads to Kent, where the couple are given a wicker class to help them create the 1930s style they want

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Graham Hartridge and Maggie Rogers have been together for 18 months and fancy an adventure. He would love to buy a place in the south-west of France, but she is nervous of taking the plunge and would prefer Devon - much closer to family and friends. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin help with their property search

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Diane Rollisson and Robert Somerville want a holiday home, but can't agree on whether it should be in Northumberland or the Midi-Pyrenees. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin show the couple places in both locations to help them decide

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: A couple want a rural holiday home, but are divided as to where. John McMahon wants to buy a place in the Llyn Peninsula in north Wales, where his rowing club is based, while his wife Elaine would prefer the warm climate and cultured atmosphere of France's Cote d'Azur. Laura Hamilton and Adrian Simpson help them to resolve the dilemma

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp goes property hunting in the trendy suburb of Heaton in Newcastle, to help Tom and Liz find a new home as they are tired of renting flats. Elsewhere, Phil Spencer is in the Tyne Valley searching for a family home for couple Michael and Sorrel, who only have two months left in their current rental property

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud investigates an innovative scheme launched by a Birmingham housing association, aimed at allowing people who cannot afford to buy their own homes to build them instead. The presenter questions the feasibility of unskilled men and women constructing habitable houses

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hunting enthusiast Hunni Hindley hosts the first party in Lincoln, basing it on a murder-mystery theme and concocting an unconventional menu in the hope of landing the £1,000 prize. There is a heated debate on hunting, and the other guests provide interesting discourse including Ricky Summers-Calvert, who has a revelation to make about his acting career

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Medical supplier Caroline Bevis hosts the second dinner party in Lincoln, hoping her Roaring Twenties-themed evening, including black-bottom Betty torte with beetroot ice-cream, will secure her the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Insurance underwriter Glenn Priestly hosts the third dinner party from Lincoln, and hopes his theme of rock music legends will win over his guests. However, the tone of the evening is lowered by student Richard Summers-Calvert's inappropriate questions

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Bodybuilding drama student Richard Summers-Calvert hosts the fourth party of the week from Lincoln. He serves chicken and rice for his starter, main and dessert, but with a few twists, and entertains his guests with a game of charades after asking them to dress up as action heroes

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: All-American mother and former barrister Natashka Ingham hosts the final dinner party from Lincolnshire, and hopes to impress her guests with a theme of The Great American Dream. Her dishes include 'fall off your horse black bottom pie' and 'cow tail pie' and, despite the fact conversation turns to underpants and toilets, she hopes to have done enough to win the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is Nateby Inn in Cumbria, where landlady Gaynor Walker is ready with a warm welcome. A trip to Gaynor's local brewery turns out to be a barrel of laughs when one guest beats another in a cask-rolling competition, before a broken cooker creates chaos in the kitchen the following morning

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Outfields Farm Bed and Breakfast in Ravensden, Bedfordshire, on their second stay, where proprietors Graham and Julia Ashley treat them to an afternoon of indoor sky-diving before hosting a dinner filled with surprises. The guests enjoy a good night's sleep, but one of the owners is expecting hassle at breakfast time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to At Home in Chiswick, west London, where artist Bob Osbourne appears to take a rather laid-back approach to running his B&B. On arrival, guests are pleasantly surprised by their first impressions of the communal living space-come-mini art gallery - but it's not long before they are dishing the dirt

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition draws to a close at the Ammonite Lodge guesthouse in Chard, Somerset, where owners Andrew and Clare Ashmore hope to excite their guests with a game of skittles. During the final dinner of the week, the competitors discuss their first impressions of each other, and Bob and Julia share some amusing observations with the group

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners confront their critics face to face, and the winners are announced. Nateby Inn host Gaynor takes issue with Graham and Julia's reduced marks for cobwebs. The conversation becomes heated when Outfields Farm's owners tackle Gaynor's complaints in return, and Georgie jumps to their defence. Artist Bob Osborne has a long list of grievances he wants to address, and Julia is left cringing when her previous comments rebound on her

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Natasha Cargill wants to build a home shaped like two enormous periscopes in rural Norfolk, but to obtain planning permission, she has to ensure not only that the materials are sustainable, but also agree to measure the transportation used to deliver them. If these strict criteria are not met, she won't be allowed to live there, and to add to the pressure, she has just £330,000 to spend on construction and six months to complete the project. Kevin McCloud follows her progress

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Schizophrenic Graham, 75, is brought to King's College Hospital after being stabbed in the neck by a stranger on his doorstep, and needs a CT scan to reveal the extent of his wound. Former nursery nurse Pat, 89, also arrives on the ward, having been found at the bottom of the stairs at home with no recollection of how she got there, while palliative care nurse Tracey treats 72-year-old retired postman Michael, who was recently diagnosed with lung cancer

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: On a busy Saturday night at King's College Hospital, staff have to juggle the needs of elderly patients and sick children alongside those who are drunk or violent. Paramedics bring 12-year-old Grace into resus after she falls down a flight of stairs, while Sarah and Andy are concerned for their 10-month-old son Dexter, who has a high temperature and is unresponsive. Elsewhere, clinical site manager Gordon has a series of tests as a result of senior sister Jen noticing his bloodshot eye

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal takes over as Craggy Island's new milkman, but his predecessor, local womaniser Pat Mustard, returns to exact an explosive revenge on Ted for getting him sacked. Starring Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal and Ted visit the mainland, where they bump into actor Richard Wilson - and discover the hard way that he does not take kindly to being confused with his comic alter ego Victor Meldrew. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon star

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with Sean Lock and Stephen Mangan teaming up against Noel Fielding and Fay Ripley. The Brett Domino Trio are in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Schizophrenic Graham, 75, is brought to King's College Hospital after being stabbed in the neck by a stranger on his doorstep, and needs a CT scan to reveal the extent of his wound. Former nursery nurse Pat, 89, also arrives on the ward, having been found at the bottom of the stairs at home with no recollection of how she got there, while palliative care nurse Tracey treats 72-year-old retired postman Michael, who was recently diagnosed with lung cancer

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: On a busy Saturday night at King's College Hospital, staff have to juggle the needs of elderly patients and sick children alongside those who are drunk or violent. Paramedics bring 12-year-old Grace into resus after she falls down a flight of stairs, while Sarah and Andy are concerned for their 10-month-old son Dexter, who has a high temperature and is unresponsive. Elsewhere, clinical site manager Gordon has a series of tests as a result of senior sister Jen noticing his bloodshot eye

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal takes over as Craggy Island's new milkman, but his predecessor, local womaniser Pat Mustard, returns to exact an explosive revenge on Ted for getting him sacked. Starring Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A village ruin that is now pitted and scarred, a ghostly fleet of ships, a bridge to nowhere hidden in a forest and strange metal structures in a wood

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Reluctant tourist Richard Ayoade is joined by comedian Matt Lucas for a frenetic 48 hours in the Italian capital. Their first challenge is finding suitable headgear to protect from the scorching Italian sun. They then take a guided tour in vintage Fiat 500 cars, and after wending their way past the Circus Maximus finish atop Gianicolo Hill in time for the cannon being fired - a daily ritual since the 1850s. Next they head to the border of the Vatican, before experiencing the very best of modern Italian cuisine. Day two sees visits to the Trevi Fountain and the Colosseum, perhaps Rome's most incredible antiquity

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by comedian Sara Pascoe for a two-day test of the mini break merits of Valencia, Spain's third largest city. After dropping their bags at the Hotel Caro, they set off along the path of the Turia river. Richard also enrols them at paella school to make authentic and vegan versions of the dish. The next day begins with a visit to Valencia's iconic City of Arts and Sciences, home to an Imax cinema, planetarium, opera house and aquarium, though Richard and Sara forgo these attractions to try zorbing instead

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads north towards the Arctic Circle to explore the culinary delights of Reykjavik, coastal capital of Iceland. His aim is to enjoy the perfect winter break, enriched with a smorgasbord featuring the best ingredients the country has to offer - fjord-reared roast lamb, rye bread baked in volcanic sand and langoustine soup. Rick also resumes his search for the perfect cod

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond take on a challenge to find an alternative to the expensive diesel-fuelled hatchbacks driven by British police officers. Alan Carr and Justin Lee Collins take up the gauntlet as the Stars in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Mercedes-Benz CLK Black Series through its paces, and Richard Hammond tests the limits of the Audi RS6 in a race against two French skiers. James May sets the show's bravery expert a daunting challenge involving an MG Maestro, while actors Rupert Penry-Jones and Peter Firth go head to head as Stars in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade and comedian Joe Lycett head to the mini break mecca of Amsterdam. Their two-day hurtle through the city's attractions takes in culture, canals, cuisine, and other things not necessarily beginning with C. As part of their whistle-stop tour, the duo doss down in one of the city's hippest hotels, take a battery-powered boat tour, do some drawing, and peer into the world of the water bear. The brave travellers also sample some Dutch gin known as jenever, and visit the city's street organ museum

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade and actress Sally Phillips head to the Swedish capital for a fulsome two days of fun. Despite having no head for heights, the duo fearlessly sample the city's only rooftop tour, before wrestling with the concept of fika - coffee and cake. The pair also a visit to the museum established in homage to 1970s pop group Abba and attend an interactive late-night art show involving their phones. After all this, there's still time to sample some meatballs, ride on a pedalo and experience the world's first ice bar

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins his journey to the Isle of Man from Berwick-upon-Tweed, Northumberland, and examines the cross-border history of the town. Plus, he hears the story of the Pitman Painters of Ashington and learns about the perils of working on the rails in Victorian times

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second of two episodes from Balmoral, where she takes a tour of the grounds in a 1953 land rover and views archive footage of the royal family. Valuables brought in to be viewed include a collection of medals from both World Wars presented to a man who also rescued survivors from the Titanic, an art nouveau mirror and a 16th-century bottle that survived being unearthed by a digger

programme.title: The World at War

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A fuel plant in Poland built to make Nazi Germany self-sufficient by using the latest technology of the time to turn coal into oil

programme.desc: Concrete channels hidden in a mountaintop forest, a collapsing bridge, a marooned military base and the upright rusting carcass of ship

programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine
programme.desc: As Hitler's ambitions spread beyond Germany, the world drew toward another great war. This one would be fought on many battlefields, but also for the hearts and minds of the people

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: As the 800th anniversary of the signing of Magna Carta in June 1215 approaches, Tony Robinson sets out to discover the real King John. His walk across the medieval monarch's stamping grounds of Sherwood Forest and the Peak District reveals a hapless man stumbling from crisis to crisis who, in the end, had no choice but to sign away some of his power. In the midst of what are now cultivated fields, Tony finds the scant remains of Laxton Castle, once the control centre where strict 'forest law' was administered and where taxes and fines flooded into the royal coffers. Later, in Sherwood, he explores how the myth of Robin Hood has helped to skew the public's image of King John

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Archaeological series. In this special edition, Tony Robinson traces the history of the controversial Sevso treasure

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson joins an unprecedented archaeological expedition to Belgium in search of a perfectly preserved WWI bunker called the Vampire.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates conflicting accounts of the killing of Italian dictator Benito Mussolini in 1945

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second of two episodes from Balmoral, where she takes a tour of the grounds in a 1953 land rover and views archive footage of the royal family. Valuables brought in to be viewed include a collection of medals from both World Wars presented to a man who also rescued survivors from the Titanic, an art nouveau mirror and a 16th-century bottle that survived being unearthed by a digger

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Concrete channels hidden in a mountaintop forest, a collapsing bridge, a marooned military base and the upright rusting carcass of ship

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the second leg of his journey from Berwick-upon-Tweed to the Isle of Man, beginning in the Northumberland village of Bardon Mill. He follows in the footsteps of Victorian archaeologists at Hadrian's Wall, discovers how the introduction of ticket machines transformed 19th-century train travel and learns about the impact of railways on the west Cumbrian town of Wigton

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Cumbria, where he visits the Sellafield site that reprocesses fuel from nuclear reactors, drinks a Victorian brew made from the waters of Cockermouth and explores a Japanese-inspired garden created in the 19th century

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a Christmas special from Lyme Park in Cheshire, where she and toy expert Will Farmer look back at children's Christmas presents dating back to Edwardian times. In the house's gardens, over 2000 visitors have arrived to have their items valued, including many with a royal heritage. The programme features a cup and saucer once owned by the Queen, a pram from Buckingham Palace, and a dagger believed to have been owned by Charles I. Plus, a celebration of the works of Charles Dickens

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Three rare Alvises get the auction buyers going when put under the hammer. A Chevy Corvette which started its journey in Saudi Arabia makes its way to Thornton le Dale for the sale

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Unusual rail networks designed to traverse water, including the Indian Ocean, the earthquake zones of the Bosphorus Strait and the sea cliffs of the Italian Riviera

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A towering structure with an explosive past, a deserted cityscape, a mysterious top-secret facility and a vast Nazi construction

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Featuring Churchill's plan to create a giant ship out of ice, ghost planes that fly themselves, and a Nazi treasure hunt

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Luftwaffe's bombing raids on Britain's provincial cities, when Coventry and Plymouth were hardest hit, although Portsmouth, Glasgow, Sheffield and Bristol also suffered heavy casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins his journey in Kirkby-in-Furness, Cumbria, where he learns about the village's famous blue slates. He then heads to Barrow to examine the secret world of nuclear submarines and finishes his trip in Lancaster, where he discovers why executions at the castle drew so many spectators in Victorian times

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ends his journey across northern England by setting sail from Heysham, Lancashire, to visit the Isle of Man, where he learns about the horse-drawn trams of Douglas, reveals the 19th-century secrets of the Laxey Waterwheel and explores the Victorian history of the Snaefell mountain railway

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox continues his exploration of the origins of mankind by investigating the matter from which everything on Earth is formed. At a Hindu cremation in Nepal, he discusses the faith's philosophy of life as an eternal cycle of creation and destruction, and draws a parallel with the lives of the universe's stars - before revealing why everything on Earth was once a part of something else

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Antiques dealer and expert restorer Gary Wallis and his team of highly skilled craftspeople turn the tired and unloved items into objects that will turn a large profit. In the opening episode, they restore a Grenadier guard's drum, create a garden feature from a helicopter tripod and re-purpose a vintage car grille into a gleaming design classic

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: While America had Ford or Chrysler or Buick, Hitler also wanted a car that would transform his nation - the 'people's car', or Volkswagen

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A towering structure with an explosive past, a deserted cityscape, a mysterious top-secret facility and a vast Nazi construction

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Luftwaffe's bombing raids on Britain's provincial cities, when Coventry and Plymouth were hardest hit, although Portsmouth, Glasgow, Sheffield and Bristol also suffered heavy casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: Featuring Churchill's plan to create a giant ship out of ice, ghost planes that fly themselves, and a Nazi treasure hunt

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes up from Lyme Regis on Dorset's Jurassic Coast to the Somerset Levels in the footsteps of a ragtag rebel army led by the young Duke of Monmouth, the illegitimate son of Charles II. He sailed in from Holland around Lyme's famous Cobb with a handful of soldiers, believing the people would support him in overthrowing the newly crowned James II, who was also Monmouth's uncle, but, more importantly, Roman Catholic. The year was 1685 and the country was riven by anti-Catholic feeling mixed up with revolutionary zeal. Monmouth marched through the fiercely independent towns of Devon, gathering support from artisans and farmworkers. Meeting up with Billy Bragg in Colyton, Tony learns about the kind of people prepared to risk their lives for the Monmouth Rebellion

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores nature's evolutionary anomalies and examines two creatures whose stretched features have given them an edge in the natural world. The chameleon has an extra-long tongue that helps it catch prey and the giraffe's neck is so long it is easily able to reach the tops of trees for food

programme.title: Viking Dead
programme.desc: How a well-preserved Viking ship discovered in 1904 continues to present archaeologists with new mysteries to unravel

programme.title: How The Beatles Changed The World
programme.desc: Through exclusive interviews and a wealth of rarely seen footage, this film reveals the previously untold story of how four young men from Liverpool transformed the world forever

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the work of the train operating company's staff along the route between London, south-west England and south Wales. The first edition focuses on problems caused by last winter's storms, with the worst weather for a century battering the nation and pushing employees and passengers to the limit. Network Rail's Steve Hawkins and his engineers have their work cut out after the line at Dawlish in Devon is washed away, while Dave Slater and his team at Network Control have to battle the elements to keep trains running as storm after storm floods the network

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: As rugby union's Six Nations Championship kicks off in Cardiff, train manager Jodie Edwards and her team try to get thousands of fans home after the match. Penzance in Cornwall remains shut off from the rest of the network as engineers work around the clock to get services running through Dawlish, where the line was washed away, while new duty manager Joe has his hands full on his first Saturday night shift at London's Paddington station when the last Wales-bound train of the evening has a problem

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Cheltenham Spa station manager Richard Morrish braces himself for a hectic four days with the two-platform station used by more than 60,000 horse racing fans during the Cheltenham Festival. Just as the mainline at Dawlish is about to be reopened after being washed away in the winter storms, a crack is discovered in the sandstone cliffs two miles down the line and a landslide threatens to engulf the tracks. Conductor Jameil and driver Gary have to react quickly on the Reading to Gatwick service when a pedestrian takes a perilous short cut

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: The first of three programmes in which cameras explore some of the most inhospitable places on the planet and the people who live there, including a team of Sherpa roping a route on Mount Everest's notorious Khumbu Icefall and, on the slopes of Mount Kilimanjaro, farmers fight pitched battles to save their crops from marauding elephants. In the Amazon rainforest, two boys undergo a rite of passage involving hundreds of ants with the most painful stings on Earth, and conservationists in the Grand Canyon try to ensure the survival of one of America's few surviving condor chicks

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: Documentary profiling some of the world's most expansive - and inhospitable - landscapes, and the people who live there. A fisherman in Zambia faces off against crocodiles and risks being swept to his death as he tries to make a living above the imposing Victoria Falls, and in the Amazon, a giant fish offers salvation to a hungry family that calls the river home. A boy's future hangs on the outcome of a single horse race in the vast grasslands of the Steppe, and in a European salt marsh, a young man duels with a savage bull in a centuries-old battle of wills

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: A man in Borneo must provide for his family by climbing to the roof of a vast cave to collect birds' nests and a Bangladeshi father and son brave deadly bees and tigers to find honey

programme.title: Prince Philip: The Plot to Make a King
programme.desc: Secret History documentary telling the inside story of the dynastic tensions that were unleashed when Princess Elizabeth, the future queen, fell in love with Prince Philip. It was felt that Philip was rough and ill-mannered - and with the Second World War still fresh in most people's minds, the royal and political elite disliked his German roots. But following the death of King George VI in 1952, the establishment feared a dynastic coup, with the Queen torn between the two sides. The documentary examines the complexities and frustrations of the royal marriage, as the monarch struggled to reconcile the love of her husband with the suspicions of her family and her government

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The crew of HMS Queen Elizabeth completes the vessel's sea trials, testing an innovative new firefighting system for the first time and marking the 100th anniversary of a plane being successfully landed on a moving ship. The carrier returns to Portsmouth, which will be its home port for the next 50 years, while the pilots of its fighter jets go to the US for additional training

programme.desc: The Iron Resurrection team tears into a '62 Chevy Truck. Joe Martin wants to create a custom Lowrider so radical it scrapes the ground.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: The documentary series follows the step-by-step processes involved in the making of three very different items - contact lenses, bread and aluminium foil

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: Designers and craftsmen reveal how CDs, tights and mozzarella cheese are produced, charting the changes in their manufacture due to technological progress

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Flamboyant Robin Lockwood hosts the first dinner party in Sheffield, planning a traditional British menu that includes a freshly shot grouse. However, animal rights activist and vegan Donna Thomas is horrified by the dish, while theatrical photographer Mark Feakins suspects the meal will prove unimpressive

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beautician Rochelle Barrett throws the second party in Sheffield, hoping to impress her guests with a Caribbean-themed meal. However, the menu provokes mixed reactions from the diners, with Robin Lockwood claiming it to be inappropriate for a dinner party. Elsewhere, vegan Donna Thomas makes a shocking discovery

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Vegan activist Donna Thomas hosts the third dinner party in Sheffield, and gets a mixed reception to her Japanese-themed menu. Rochelle worries about the alcohol-free night, and Sarah dislikes tofu, but Mark is excited about the deep-fried ice-cream dessert

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Theatrical photographer Mark Feakins hosts the penultimate dinner party in Sheffield, and decides to play a devious wine trick on his guests. Sarah and Robin continue their flirtatious banter, while Rochelle and Donna are concerned about one of the dishes

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: GP Sarah Roscoe hosts the final dinner party in Sheffield, hoping to impress her guests with a gourmet menu. She also tries to win over the other amateur chefs with her extensive wine selection, and her decision to cook veal sparks mixed reactions, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps a couple find a holiday home on the Greek island of Crete, hoping to find a traditional village house on a budget of £80,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Reality TV star and choreographer Louie Spence and his husband Leto Fernandez have long-dreamed of owning a holiday home in Spain's Almeria Province - somewhere to spend the winter months and short summer breaks with friends and family. Laura Hamilton is on hand to show them five properties within their £185,000 budget, but will any of them have Louie high-kicking with delight?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Katharine and Rory offer some proper Yorkshire hospitality at Chapter Two Accommodation in Malton, including an afternoon of sausage-making and a belly-busting breakfast

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Woking, where they find a sidecar to be transformed into a boat and a water cylinder that Simon might make a trendy lamp

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A friendly woman comes to Frasier's rescue when he's out Christmas shopping. He offers to return the favour, but the last thing he expects is to end up on a blind date with her daughter - while pretending to be Jewish. Meanwhile, Niles takes part in a festive pageant

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A series of good deeds backfires, embroiling Frasier in a humiliating scandal and causing him to question his faith in human nature. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of a restaurant in Mount Sinai, New York, which appears to be stuck in the 1980s. The crumbling decor and unenthusiastic head chef at the Handlebar need to be tackled, but owners Bill and Carolyn LeRoy are in denial about the problems they face

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to the Park's Edge restaurant in Atlanta, Georgia, where a series of spats with the local community are hampering its success. Owners Richard and Jorge's in-fighting has alienated locals, and the duo's suggestion that customer numbers are low because of racism has heightened tensions

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps a family from North Shields search for a property in the picturesque coastal town of La Duquesa on Spain's Costa del Sol

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sarah from Wiltshire looks for a three-bedroom house in Bulgaria that falls within her budget of £40,000, and Jonnie Irwin finds five properties for her to choose from

programme.title: Phil Spencer's Stately Homes
programme.desc: The property expert returns to his native Kent to visit 900-year-old Leeds Castle. Despite visiting many times as a child, Phil discovers there is a lot more to this history-laden fort than he ever imagined as he sets out to discover how it was built, who made their mark on it and how much it cost

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the demands placed on the women and men who answer people's cries for help. As an ambulance crew answers a call to a heart attack, call handlers guide the victim's daughter through administering CPR. A nine-year-old boy is coached through a terrifying situation when his mother suffers a diabetic emergency while driving, and a father-to-be is instructed how to deliver his baby over the phone

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Italian restaurant-owner Giovanni, 61, who has been single for nearly 20 years meets 54-year-old plumber's mate June. Other dating diners include Tyrone, a 33-year-old prison officer with ADHD. He meets Jason, a retail manager who wants some fire in his life

programme.title: Crime and Punishment
programme.desc: With more than 700 children currently serving prison sentences across England and Wales, the documentary asks why Britain struggles to stop young offenders being sucked into a life of crime. DS Will Whale and his team at Southampton CID investigate 25 burglaries from elderly people's homes, crimes they suspect are connected to a group of teenagers. In Winchester Prison, a young inmate is sent to the segregation unit after climbing onto the roof, while in Hampshire, a probation officer is doing everything she can to keep a prolific offender on the straight and narrow

programme.title: Lose a Stone in 21 Days with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: The doctor puts five volunteers on a low-calorie diet to help get their health back on track after the UK's Covid-19 lockdown. In the third and final week of the diet challenge, Michael explores the connection between comfort eating, and the different ways our brains respond when faced with a bar of chocolate as opposed to a pile of fruit and vegetables. He also has the unique experience of having a camera stuck up his nose to learn about the connection between weight, snoring and the life-limiting condition of sleep apnoea

programme.title: How to Beat
programme.desc: Kate Quilton and Dr Javid Abdelmoneim continue their health check of the UK, enlisting 10 stressed-out volunteers, ranging from a firefighter and a paramedic to a chef, to find out if simple lifestyle changes can alleviate stress. Is it as simple as cutting back alcohol and caffeine, while taking up hobbies and practising mindfulness meditation? Kate also visits Lego, a company leading the way on employee wellbeing, Javid shows how spending time in green spaces can reduce stress levels, and there's a look at pioneering treatment for people with phobias

programme.title: The Yorkshire Jobcentre
programme.desc: When anyone and everyone can come through their door, the dedicated team of no-nonsense job coaches at the busy public employment service always try to go the extra mile for customers, changing lives every day. Gaz, 17, has been referred to the social justice team as he is about to become homeless, while single mum Olivia, 29, has dreams of setting up her own business making ethnically diverse dolls. Also featured is Rose, who has just arrived in Leeds and is new to life on Universal Credit

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Nurse Karen and GP Alastair from Montrose long to relocate to the beautiful countryside of the Dordogne in France. Laura Hamilton shows them five options for their £175,000 budget

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Shoukry Rozek welcomes his rivals to Four Saints Brig Y Don in Llandudno, north Wales, where the sea views impress some guests, although the buffet breakfast proves controversial

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Self-proclaimed princess Tracey Durrant hosts the first dinner party in Stoke-on-Trent. She aims to use the finest ingredients, and prepares the food as quickly as possible so she has plenty of time to pamper herself and her two pet dogs before the guests arrive

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Police officer Claire Crank hosts the second round of the contest in Stoke-on-Trent, and insists on stripping the event to the basics of good food. However, Tracey Durrant declares she only likes the best-quality beef, and determines to refuse to eat the hostess's offerings before she has even arrived at the party

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Recruitment manager Rob McNamara presents the third dinner party in Stoke-on-Trent. Despite rarely cooking and never having played host before, he is confident he has a chance of winning the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty parlour owner Michelle Minter delivers a gourmet menu at the penultimate dinner party in Stoke-on-Trent, featuring crostini with pork fillet and mushroom pate, and a twice-baked souffle. The atmosphere is tense after last night's argument, so everyone is relieved when Tracey gets stuck in traffic and they have to start without her

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: City councillor Matt Wilcox hosts the final dinner party in Stoke-on-Trent, hoping to impress with unusual dishes including a bucket of bones and his spicy twist on a local stew. He also lines up musical entertainment and lets an artist decorate the table, while Michelle tries to get an apology from Tracey before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps a family from North Shields search for a property in the picturesque coastal town of La Duquesa on Spain's Costa del Sol

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Anne Hunt from Cheshire has recently retired and has long dreamed of buying a property in Spain where she can spend her new-found free time relaxing in the sun. With her best friend Sharon along to help her with the decision, Danni Menzies shows her five properties on the southern Costa Blanca for her £50,000 budget

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Tanya defends the lack of breakfast offering at Greenview Guesthouse and is left flabbergasted when the payments are revealed

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Herefordshire to meet former farmer Julian. Henry finds an old cattle feeder that he wants to turn into a garden bench, while Simon hatches plans to turn a vintage seed drill into a trendy dining table

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Roz helps Frasier arrange a date between her mother and Martin, unaware they detest each other. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Peri Gilpin

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Woody pays Frasier a surprise visit and the friends become nostalgic about old times in a certain Boston bar. With a guest appearance by Woody Harrelson

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to Italian family restaurant Sabatiello's in Stamford, Connecticut, where the owner's fiery temper upsets the staff and has left him more than $1million in debt. Gordon's help is urgently needed to get customers back through the door, but the restaurateur does not take kindly to criticism and closes the restaurant down when his employees turn on him

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to the Greek at the Harbour restaurant in southern California, where owner Makis Mikelatos works 17 hours a day, seven days a week, but is unwilling to let his son Ari take the reins. Gordon sets out to get to the bottom of Makis's reluctance to teach his son, and uncovers a shocking family secret

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Nurse Karen and GP Alastair from Montrose long to relocate to the beautiful countryside of the Dordogne in France. Laura Hamilton shows them five options for their £175,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas is house-hunting in Torremolinos on the Costa Del Sol with Chris and Jacqueline from Lancashire, finding five properties within their £150,000 budget

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Labrador puppy with an elbow disease, and believes a possible solution might involve fitted plates and stem cell injections. Elsewhere, a one-year-old cat is brought in suffering from a crushed pelvis and a broken tail after a road traffic accident, and a Russian Black Terrier with a cancerous growth is at risk of losing his leg

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mark, a 35-year-old long-term resident at a neurological hospital, is rushed to St George's struggling to breathe, and his devoted parents are with him as doctors attempt to get to the bottom of his symptoms. Three-year-old Thomas is brought in with a high temperature by his parents, who are concerned as he was born with an extremely rare blood condition that meant he needed a full blood exchange when he was less than 24 hours old

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Greenville, Mississippi, to save fine dining establishment Sherman's Restaurant, which has been a presence in the town for 30 years. The chef and his team have just 24 hours to revamp the premises, clean and renovate the kitchen and introduce a new menu featuring update versions of the restaurant's staple dishes, working around the clock to teach the staff how to prepare them

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: A compilation of the best bits from recent series with host Jimmy Carr, team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, and Countdown regulars Susie Dent and Rachel Riley, joined by a host of top comedians including Alan Carr, Richard Ayoade, Kathy Burke, Roisin Conaty, Joe Wilkinson, Johnny Vegas, Katherine Ryan, Phil Wang, Aisling Bea and David Mitchell

programme.title: Derren Brown: Apocalypse
programme.desc: He survived a game of live Russian roulette, 'predicted' the lottery numbers, stuck half the viewing audience to their sofas and taught a member of the public to fly a plane overnight. Now, acclaimed showman Derren Brown presents a two-part special that is arguably his most audacious stunt yet, as he convinces one person that the planet has been devastated by a meteor strike and that he is only one of a handful of survivors. Will the chosen one rise to the challenge?

programme.title: Derren Brown: Apocalypse
programme.desc: The acclaimed showman concludes his two-part special in which he convinces one person they have survived a meteor strike on Earth, which is now a zombie-ridden land. The unwitting participant continues his adventure in a post-apocalyptic world as a series of challenges and staged situations begins to transform the way he sees himself and his life

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: Make Do and Mend
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge help Natalie and Pete come up with some compact camping ideas, such as a portable solar shower that doubles up as a sink and tyre chairs with a rustic rope finish. Dick also helps a viewer transform her garden into a plot she can be proud of, and Angel offers upholstery advice to a woman whose favourite chair has been left in tatters by the family dog

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Kamlesh and Sukhwinder have been married for 42 years, and now their four children have flown the nest, wish to buy a winter home in southern Alicante, with its breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea and lively atmosphere. Jean Johansson shows them five properties for their £95,000 budget, but whether any will take their fancy remains to be seen

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners are meeting for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. The Hero is first, and hosts Harry and Rowan Glennie want to know why their female guests thought their rooms were too boyish to cut the mustard

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking Family Favourites
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates recipes for simple, affordable and delicious meals, from quick and tasty dinners to useful staples to add to regular repertoires. The chef kicks off by teaming chicken with flavour-packed Cumberland sausages and tasty veggies to creating a one-pan roast dinner, and puts a British twist on the classic spaghetti Bolognese by swapping in pale ale and Cheddar cheese to create an easy, cheap and tasty batch-cook recipe. Plus, he's got a fun take on pasta preparation that the kids - and adults - will love

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Grandmother Angie hosts the first of a week of dinner parties from in and around Edinburgh, hoping to impress Abby, Marc, Gareth and Celena with her menu of international dishes

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Creative director Gareth seems to be suffering from big night nerves as he hosts the second party from Edinburgh - which involves a curry with an unappetising name

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ex-restaurant owner Celena hosts the third dinner party of the week in and around Edinburgh, hoping to impress her guests with her Italian-inspired menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth day in Edinburgh, retail manager Marc hopes to impress his guests with a bug-based menu. But before long the tension between Angie and Abby takes centre stage again

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Funeral arranger Abby is last to host in Edinburgh. She treats her guests to a combination of healthy and Spanish grub, but will partying pensioner Angie steal the limelight? Creative director Gareth gets to tell ex-restaurant manager Celena what he really thinks of her, and golfing supplier Marc proves what a Scotsman really wears under his kilt

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole visit Witney in Oxfordshire, where Henry comes across a tractor that has not run for quite a few years - but poses no problem for restorer Alan

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole makes a table out of a part from a jet in Tiverton, while Simon O'Brien finds a pair of exhaust pipes which he thinks could become a trendy pair of lamps

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Worcestershire to meet mechanic Adrian. Meanwhile, Simon restores an old, wrecked armchair into some punk-rock furniture

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel to Surrey to meet upcycler Rob, who has a range of sheds a few miles from Gatwick Airport

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Wokingham in Berkshire to meet professional gardener Mark whose barns are brimful of bikes, bric-a-brac and baggage

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef comes to the aid of Anthony Trobiano, whose restaurant in Great Neck, New York, is failing even though his partner's parents have given him their life savings and he has taken out half a million dollars in loans. Gordon has some harsh criticism about the unhygienic kitchen and the quality of the food being served, but the owner has trouble accepting his advice

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding present the first in a series of special charity editions of the culinary challenge, with judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith. programme.title: Derren Brown: 20 Years of Mind Control
programme.desc: The illusionist performs his latest psychological trick live, and also revisits some of the most memorable moments of his 20 years of making programmes for Channel 4. He reveals how he has used hypnosis and the power of suggestion to baffle the British public, before opening up about his most controversial, headline-grabbing moments, including playing Russian roulette, 'sticking' viewers to their sofas, and the zombie-fuelled Apocalypse illusion

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Canal boat stewardess Denise Hendon hosts the first dinner party in Birmingham. While party girl Angela McFarlane dreads eating the couscous, suave Frenchman Jean-Benoit Burloux longs for a glass of fine wine to wash the meal down

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Daniela Lawler throws the second party in Birmingham, hoping to impress her guests by inviting them to the Georgian country house she shares with her husband, DJ Steve Lawler. The hostess aims high by planning a four-course Italian-inspired meal, and everything seems to be going well, until she realises her lobster is too big to fit in her pans

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Office administrator Angela McFarlane hosts the third dinner party in Birmingham, and hopes to impress with a starter of chicken and spring onion terrine with red onion marmalade. However, the guests seem more interested in rummaging through her bedroom drawers

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Electrician Elliot Jauncey hosts the penultimate dinner party in Birmingham, and despite a lack of experience, plans a top-class menu featuring two desserts. He also books a local band to serenade his guests, but the evening turns out to be far more difficult than he had expected

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jean-Benoit Burloux hosts the final dinner party in Birmingham, and plans a four-course menu including crab in a bird's nest, a blade of beef and a pavlova with raspberries. programme.title: Teleshopping
